addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Supabase
Here are guidelines for using Supabase tools effectively:

- Before making schema changes, use `list_tables` to understand the existing structure
- When debugging issues, start with `get_logs` and `get_advisors` before making changes
- Use `get_project_url` and `get_publishable_api_key` when helping users configure client-side integrations

If you have access to a local development environment with a filesystem and shell:
- Install the Supabase agent skill for critical development and security guidance: `npx skills add supabase/agent-skills` (https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/ai-skills.md)
- Use the Supabase CLI (`supabase`) for local development workflows such as starting a local stack, managing migrations, and running edge functions locally (https://supabase.com/docs/guides/local-development.md)
- Prefer local development and testing before applying changes to a remote project

If you are running in a web-only or remote environment without filesystem or shell access:
- Rely on the MCP tools directly for all Supabase interactions
- Use `apply_migration` carefully, as changes go directly to the remote project

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# CRM Backend Migration Plan: Directus To Shared Supabase
2	
3	This document is the execution plan for migrating `u2giants/popcrm-web` from the
4	current Directus backend to the shared enterprise-wide Supabase backend.
5	
6	It is written for a fresh AI/developer session with no prior chat context. Read
7	this file completely before making changes.
8	
9	## Executive Summary
10	
11	The CRM app currently reads and writes directly to a shared Directus API at
12	`https://data.designflow.app`. The new strategy is not to create a CRM-specific
13	Supabase project. Instead, CRM, PM/PIM, DAM, and operational PLM data will live
14	in one shared Supabase.com project with logical schemas.
15	
16	The CRM migration has two parts:
17	
18	1. Backend/data-layer work in the canonical shared database repo.
19	2. Frontend and worker rewrites in this CRM repo and the existing CRM worker.
20	
21	Do not create a separate Supabase project for CRM.
22	
23	Do not put permanent database DDL in this repo.
24	
25	Do not run unreviewed SQL directly against production.
26	
27	## Canonical Shared-DB References
28	
29	The shared database plan is maintained in `u2giants/shared-db` and mirrored into
30	consumer repos under `shared-db/`. Mirrored copies are read-only and overwritten
31	by sync.
32	
33	If the local checkout does not contain `shared-db/`, use the GitHub links below.
34	
35	Primary shared-db docs:
36	
37	- [Shared DB README](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/tree/main/shared-db)
38	- [Shared database vision](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/shared-database-vision.md)
39	- [Unified Supabase schema map](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md)
40	- [Unified Supabase relationships](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-relationships.md)
41	- [Unified Supabase migration gaps](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-migration-gaps.md)
42	- [Supabase migration preparation](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/supabase-migration-prep.md)
43	- [Schema implementation notes](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/implementation/schema-implementation-notes.md)
44	- [Preview branch verification](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/verification/preview-branch-20260621.md)
45	- [Shared Supabase branch workflow](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/ai-session-instructions/shared-supabase-branch-workflow.md)
46	- [CRM-specific Supabase migration guide](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/ai-session-instructions/popcrm-web-supabase-migration.md)
47	- [AI session instructions index](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/ai-session-instructions/README.md)
48	
49	Baseline SQL migrations in shared-db:
50	
51	- [20260621000100_foundation.sql](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260621000100_foundation.sql)
52	- [20260621000200_app_core.sql](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260621000200_app_core.sql)
53	- [20260621000300_domain_tables.sql](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260621000300_domain_tables.sql)
54	- [20260621000400_api_rls_realtime.sql](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260621000400_api_rls_realtime.sql)
55	
56	## Supabase Targets
57	
58	Use the shared Supabase project refs from the shared-db docs.
59	
60	Preview branch:
61	
62	```text
63	Project ref: tcscehehgeiijilylezv
64	URL: https://tcscehehgeiijilylezv.supabase.co
65	Branch name: shared-db-schema-rehearsal
66	Purpose: shared integration target for CRM/PM rewrite work
67	```
68	
69	Production/default project:
70	
71	```text
72	Project ref: qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai
73	URL: https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co
74	Purpose: live shared enterprise Supabase project; currently live PopDAM base
75	```
76	
77	Important:
78	
79	- Apply new CRM database migrations to preview first.
80	- Production promotion must happen through committed shared-db migration files.
81	- Do not manually copy SQL objects from preview to production in the Supabase dashboard.
82	- The previously discussed project ref `bhnrhaqesgomgeuppbjp` is not the active target for this shared strategy unless the owner explicitly changes the shared-db docs.
83	
84	## Current CRM Architecture
85	
86	`popcrm-web` is a Vite/React/TypeScript SPA. It stores no local business data.
87	All current business reads/writes go through the shared Directus backend at
88	`https://data.designflow.app`.
89	
90	Production app:
91	
92	```text
93	https://crm.designflow.app
94	```
95	
96	Preview alias:
97	
98	```text
99	https://crm-dev.designflow.app
100	```
101	
102	Current Directus backend:
103	
104	```text
105	https://data.designflow.app
106	```
107	
108	Current Fireflies/CRM worker endpoint:
109	
110	```text
111	https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app
112	```
113	
114	## Current Directus Dependency Surface In This Repo
115	
116	The CRM frontend is fairly well centralized around a small number of Directus
117	entry points. Migrate these deliberately.
118	
119	Files to rewrite or adapt:
120	
121	| File | Current role |
122	|---|---|
123	| `src/lib/directus.ts` | Directus SDK client, Directus URL, session-cookie auth mode, Microsoft SSO URL helper, Directus asset URL helper. |
124	| `src/auth/auth.tsx` | Directus `readMe`, login, logout, current user state. |
125	| `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` | Calls `microsoftLoginUrl()` and email/password login. |
126	| `src/features/crm/api.ts` | All CRM collection reads/writes using `@directus/sdk`. |
127	| `src/features/crm/CrmDataContext.tsx` | Bootstrap loader. Must preserve `Promise.allSettled` behavior. |
128	| `src/lib/types.ts` | Hand-maintained Directus-shaped schema slice. |
129	| `src/features/crm/constants.ts` | Enum/status logic; may need updates if Supabase values differ. |
130	
131	Search command to verify Directus usage:
132	
133	```bash
134	rg "directus|@directus/sdk|readItems|createItem|updateItem|deleteItem|readMe|microsoftLoginUrl" src -n
135	```
136	
137	Expected current direct dependencies include:
138	
139	- `src/lib/directus.ts`
140	- `src/auth/auth.tsx`
141	- `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx`
142	- `src/features/crm/api.ts`
143	- occasional constant/docs references
144	
145	## Current Directus Backend/Worker Sources
146	
147	The CRM backend schema and worker currently live in the sibling Directus repo.
148	Use these as source references while designing migrations and worker behavior.
149	
150	Relevant files:
151	
152	| File | Purpose |
153	|---|---|
154	| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-schema.mjs` | Directus CRM schema source: collections, fields, relations, enums. |
155	| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-roles.mjs` | Current Directus role grants for CRM collections. |
156	| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-worker.mjs` | Outlook ingest, reroute, Fireflies webhook, contact sync, summaries, ignore-rule application, opportunity chat. |
157	| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/sql/enforce-crm-department-scope.sql` | SQL trigger logic enforcing department belongs to selected customer. |
158	| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/migration/split-customers-from-ingested.sql` | Current split between curated customer tables and raw ingested registries. |
159	
160	If `/worksp/directus` is unavailable in a fresh environment, clone or inspect the
161	`u2giants/directus` repo before writing backend migrations.
162	
163	## Current CRM Collections And Shared Supabase Targets
164	
165	Do not carry Directus collection names forward blindly. The shared database uses
166	canonical schemas.
167	
168	| Current Directus collection | Shared Supabase target | Notes |
169	|---|---|---|
170	| `retailer` | `core.company` | Curated customers/accounts. One canonical company table shared by CRM, PM, DAM, and PLM. |
171	| `ingested_domains` | `core.company_source_ref` and/or `ingest.raw_record` | Raw ingested company/domain registry. Do not expose directly to broad browser contracts. |
172	| `buyer` | `core.contact`, `core.contact_company` | Curated contacts/buyers. Contact-company relation carries account relationship. |
173	| `ingested_contact` | `core.contact_source_ref` and/or `ingest.raw_record` | Raw ingested contact registry. Do not expose directly to broad browser contracts. |
174	| `factory` | `core.factory` | Canonical factory/vendor identity shared with PM and PLM. |
175	| `project` | `pim.project` | PM/PIM project. CRM opportunity may link to it. |
176	| `crm_department` | `crm.department` | CRM-owned account department segmentation, linked to `core.company`. |
177	| `crm_opportunity` | `crm.opportunity` | CRM-owned sales/program pipeline, linked to `core.company`, `core.contact`, `crm.department`, `pim.project`, and possibly PLM production orders. |
178	| `crm_email_message` | `crm.email_message` | CRM-owned communication/routing record. Raw/sensitive bodies must not be broadly exposed. |
179	| `crm_meeting_note` | `crm.meeting_note` | CRM-owned meeting notes and Fireflies-derived summaries. |
180	| `crm_ignore_rule` | `crm.ignore_rule` | CRM-owned routing skip rules. |
181	| `crm_ai_model_config` | `crm.ai_model_config` | CRM-owned model settings. |
182	| `crm_note` | `crm.note` or later `app.comment` | Keep in `crm.note` initially unless the note becomes cross-domain. |
183	| `crm_task` | `crm.task` | CRM-owned task workflow. |
184	| `crm_licensor_approval_thread` | `crm.licensor_approval_thread` | CRM approval workflow; can link to PM submissions/revisions where known. |
185	| `directus_users` | `app.profile` | Supabase Auth owns identity; app profile owns business profile data. |
186	
187	Hard rule:
188	
189	Do not create duplicate `crm.company`, `crm.contact`, `crm.project`,
190	`crm.factory`, or CRM-only taxonomy tables. Shared identity/reference objects
191	belong in `core` or `pim`.
192	
193	## Existing Baseline Shared Schema
194	
195	The shared-db baseline migrations already implement these logical schemas:
196	
197	```text
198	app
199	core
200	dam
201	pim
202	crm
203	plm
204	ingest
205	api
206	```
207	
208	Important baseline objects:
209	
210	```text
211	app.profile
212	app.role
213	app.user_role
214	app.app_access
215	app.comment
216	app.activity
217	app.notification
218	app.file_object
219	
220	core.company
221	core.company_source_ref
222	core.contact
223	core.contact_source_ref
224	core.contact_company
225	core.licensor
226	core.property
227	core.character
228	core.factory
229	core.factory_source_ref
230	core.product_category
231	core.product_type
232	core.product_subtype
233	core.sku_ref
234	
235	crm.department
236	crm.opportunity
237	crm.opportunity_product
238	crm.email_message
239	crm.meeting_note
240	crm.note
241	crm.task
242	crm.ignore_rule
243	crm.ai_model_config
244	crm.licensor_approval_thread
245	
246	pim.project
247	pim.product
248	pim.product_submission
249	pim.revision_request
250	
251	ingest.sync_run
252	ingest.raw_record
253	ingest.dedupe_candidate
254	
255	api.crm_account_overview
256	api.global_search
257	```
258	
259	The baseline is intentionally first-pass. It does not necessarily cover every
260	field or screen contract needed by the current CRM frontend.
261	
262	## High-Level Migration Phases
263	
264	Do the work in this order:
265	
266	1. Read shared-db docs and current CRM code.
267	2. Audit current Directus collection fields and CRM frontend field usage.
268	3. Add missing shared-db migrations on the preview branch.
269	4. Build API views/RPCs for CRM screen contracts.
270	5. Design/import data from Directus into shared schemas.
271	6. Rewrite CRM frontend auth and API access to Supabase.
272	7. Port the CRM worker to Supabase service-role access.
273	8. Test everything against the preview branch.
274	9. Promote migrations to production through shared-db.
275	10. Cut over CRM frontend and worker.
276	11. Keep Directus read-only for rollback/reference during the stabilization period.
277	
278	## Phase 1: Read And Audit
279	
280	Start by reading the shared-db docs listed above. Then inspect the CRM frontend.
281	
282	Commands:
283	
284	```bash
285	pwd
286	rg "directus|@directus/sdk|readItems|createItem|updateItem|deleteItem|readMe|microsoftLoginUrl" src -n
287	sed -n '1,260p' src/features/crm/api.ts
288	sed -n '1,260p' src/lib/types.ts
289	sed -n '1,220p' src/auth/auth.tsx
290	sed -n '1,220p' src/lib/directus.ts
291	sed -n '1,260p' src/features/crm/CrmDataContext.tsx
292	```
293	
294	If available, inspect the Directus backend source:
295	
296	```bash
297	sed -n '1,460p' /worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-schema.mjs
298	sed -n '1,260p' /worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-roles.mjs
299	sed -n '1,1120p' /worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-worker.mjs
300	sed -n '1,180p' /worksp/directus/pm-system/sql/enforce-crm-department-scope.sql
301	sed -n '1,220p' /worksp/directus/pm-system/migration/split-customers-from-ingested.sql
302	```
303	
304	Deliverable from this phase:
305	
306	- A field-level inventory of every Directus field the CRM frontend or worker reads/writes.
307	- A gap list comparing those fields to existing shared-db baseline tables.
308	- A list of screens that can use existing `api` views and screens requiring new views/RPCs.
309	
310	## Phase 2: Shared-DB Migration Workflow
311	
312	All database changes go in `u2giants/shared-db`, not this repo.
313	
314	From a checkout of `u2giants/shared-db`:
315	
316	```bash
317	supabase link --project-ref tcscehehgeiijilylezv
318	scripts/check-sql.sh
319	supabase db push --dry-run
320	```
321	
322	Create new timestamped migrations for CRM-specific work:
323	
324	```text
325	supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_crm_api_contracts.sql
326	supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_crm_directus_parity_fields.sql
327	supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_crm_worker_rpc.sql
328	supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_crm_rls_tightening.sql
329	supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_crm_realtime_contracts.sql
330	```
331	
332	Naming rules:
333	
334	- Use unique timestamps.
335	- Include `crm_` in CRM-specific migration names.
336	- Keep changes additive where possible.
337	- Add comments for security-sensitive policies and RPCs.
338	
339	Apply only to preview first:
340	
341	```bash
342	scripts/check-sql.sh
343	supabase db push --dry-run
344	supabase db push
345	supabase migration list
346	```
347	
348	Never use production project ref while developing.
349	
350	## Phase 3: Shared Schema Gap Resolution
351	
352	Compare current CRM needs to baseline tables.
353	
354	Known likely gaps:
355	
356	| Current CRM need | Baseline status | Action |
357	|---|---|---|
358	| Account status values like `ACTIVE_CUSTOMER`, `POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER`, `OTHER`, `UNASSIGNED` | `core.company.status` is generic `app.entity_status`; app-specific status may need metadata or a dedicated field. | Decide whether to add `core.company.customer_status` or store Directus CRM status in `metadata`. Prefer explicit field if it drives UI and filters. |
359	| Retailer domain/routing aliases | `core.company.domain` exists; routing aliases likely not first-class. | Add explicit field or metadata contract if email routing needs it. |
360	| Buyer/contact type and scope | `core.contact.title` and `core.contact_company.relationship_type` exist; CRM-specific `contact_type`/`scope` may be missing. | Add fields to `core.contact_company` or `crm` bridge table; do not duplicate contact. |
361	| CRM department category/division/active | `crm.department` baseline has `status` and metadata only. | Add explicit fields if UI filters/edits them. |
362	| Opportunity fields `amount`, `program_type`, `season_year`, `division`, PO/SO/hard delivery, AI fields | Baseline has `estimated_value`, `production_po_number`, `sales_order_number`, metadata. | Add explicit columns for fields used broadly; keep rare/import fields in metadata. |
363	| Email routing fields `routing_status`, `routing_method`, `sender`, `recipients`, detected numbers | Baseline has `status`, `direction`, `subject`, `body_preview`, `body_storage_ref`, metadata. | Add explicit routing columns or create API view that maps metadata to expected shape. |
364	| Meeting `participants`, `action_items`, `source`, `fireflies_transcript_id` | Baseline has `title`, `body`, `meeting_at`, external refs, metadata. | Add explicit fields or define metadata contract. |
365	| Notes `title`, `action_items`, source fields | Baseline `crm.note` has `body` only. | Add columns if current UI depends on them. |
366	| Task `body`, department/contact/opportunity relations | Baseline task has company/opportunity/assignee/title/status/due. | Add missing relations/fields. |
367	| Approval free-form fields `property_name`, `stage`, dates, comments | Baseline approval thread has subject/status and links. | Add free-form stage and current date/comment columns; do not convert to enum. |
368	
369	Important principle:
370	
371	Do not force all old Directus fields into 1:1 columns. Use explicit columns for
372	fields that are queried, filtered, displayed prominently, or edited. Use
373	`metadata` for low-value historical/import fields.
374	
375	## Phase 4: API Views And RPC Contracts
376	
377	The CRM frontend should not manually stitch many raw tables for every screen.
378	Use browser-facing `api` views/RPCs for joined, RLS-safe contracts.
379	
380	Existing baseline views:
381	
382	```text
383	api.crm_account_overview
384	api.global_search
385	```
386	
387	Add new CRM contracts as needed:
388	
389	| CRM screen | Recommended contract |
390	|---|---|
391	| Overview | `api.crm_account_overview`, plus `api.crm_activity_summary` or specific rollup RPCs. |
392	| Accounts | `api.crm_account_list` |
393	| Contacts | `api.crm_contact_list` |
394	| Departments | `api.crm_department_contacts` |
395	| Pipeline | `api.crm_opportunity_board`, `api.crm_opportunity_list` |
396	| Programs | `api.crm_opportunity_list` |
397	| Email Routing | `api.crm_email_routing_queue` |
398	| Meetings | `api.crm_meeting_list` |
399	| Notes | `api.crm_note_list` |
400	| Tasks | `api.crm_task_list` |
401	| Approvals | `api.crm_approval_queue` |
402	| Settings | direct `crm.ai_model_config` or `api.crm_settings_models` |
403	| Command search | `api.global_search`, possibly extended |
404	
405	View design requirements:
406	
407	- Use `security_invoker = true`.
408	- Select only fields intended for browser use.
409	- Do not expose raw email bodies or broad raw meeting payloads.
410	- Do not expose `ingest.raw_record`.
411	- Do not expose PLM/RFQ/cost base tables.
412	- Include stable aliases that are convenient for frontend code.
413	- Keep write behavior on base tables or through RPCs, not writeable views unless deliberately designed.
414	
415	RPCs are appropriate for workflow actions that require multiple writes or
416	security checks:
417	
418	```text
419	api.crm_apply_email_route(...)
420	api.crm_create_ignore_rule_from_email(...)
421	api.crm_update_opportunity_stage(...)
422	api.crm_create_note_for_opportunity(...)
423	api.crm_assign_task(...)
424	```
425	
426	If a CRM action should update PM data immediately, use a database trigger or
427	service-side RPC/function. Do not dual-write from browser code.
428	
429	## Phase 5: Identity, Auth, App Access, And Roles
430	
431	Directus users do not transfer directly.
432	
433	Target identity model:
434	
435	```text
436	auth.users
437	app.profile
438	app.role
439	app.user_role
440	app.app_access
441	```
442	
443	Supabase Auth owns authentication. `app.profile` owns app-facing profile data.
444	`app.user_role` and `app.app_access` drive RLS helper functions.
445	
446	Expected roles:
447	
448	```text
449	administrator
450	sales
451	licensing
452	designer
453	viewer
454	vendor
455	```
456	
457	CRM access should require either:
458	
459	```sql
460	app.has_app_access('crm')
461	```
462	
463	or administrator role:
464	
465	```sql
466	app.has_role('administrator')
467	```
468	
469	Initial behavior:
470	
471	| Role | CRM access |
472	|---|---|
473	| `administrator` | Full access. |
474	| `sales` | CRM read/write for operational CRM tables. |
475	| `licensing` | CRM read/write where licensing/approval work requires it. |
476	| `designer` | No broad CRM writes unless explicitly approved. Read access only if business needs it. |
477	| `viewer` | Read-only if granted CRM app access. |
478	| `vendor` | No CRM access unless a future scoped requirement is designed. |
479	
480	Implementation tasks:
481	
482	1. Configure Microsoft/Azure provider in Supabase Auth.
483	2. Import/map Directus users into `app.profile`.
484	3. Map Directus/Entra roles into `app.role` / `app.user_role`.
485	4. Grant `app.app_access` rows for CRM users.
486	5. Decide how Entra group membership becomes Supabase role state.
487	6. Do not trust browser-provided role values for authorization.
488	
489	If Entra remains the role hub, use a server-side sync or custom access-token hook
490	strategy. Do not rely on client-side role claims without RLS verification.
491	
492	## Phase 6: RLS Requirements
493	
494	The baseline shared-db RLS is a scaffold. Tighten and test before production.
495	
496	CRM RLS requirements:
497	
498	- Authenticated CRM users can read only intended CRM data.
499	- Sales/licensing can write only intended CRM records.
500	- Viewers cannot write.
501	- Vendors cannot read CRM data unless explicitly scoped later.
502	- `ingest.*` is admin/service-only.
503	- `core.*_source_ref` tables are admin/service-only by default.
504	- Raw email/meeting payloads are not exposed through broad views.
505	- Worker/admin RPCs require service role or explicit admin checks.
506	- PLM/RFQ/cost/pricing tables are not exposed to CRM users through broad grants.
507	
508	Test with real Supabase users for each role:
509	
510	```text
511	administrator
512	sales
513	licensing
514	viewer
515	vendor
516	```
517	
518	Do not mark migration complete until role-specific reads/writes have been tested
519	against the preview branch.
520	
521	## Phase 7: Data Migration Design
522	
523	Use Directus/Postgres data as the source. Do not rely on Directus API exports as
524	the primary migration path.
525	
526	Recommended import order:
527	
528	1. Take a fresh Directus Postgres dump.
529	2. Load source rows into staging or `ingest.raw_record`.
530	3. Create source-reference rows.
531	4. Build canonical `core` rows.
532	5. Load CRM-owned `crm` rows with FKs to canonical rows.
533	6. Verify row counts and relationships.
534	7. Only then point frontend preview at the data.
535	
536	Detailed sequence:
537	
538	### 7.1 Source Dump
539	
540	Take a fresh source dump before each rehearsal.
541	
542	Source Directus database context from existing docs:
543	
544	```text
545	Production API/Data Studio: https://data.designflow.app
546	Source database: Postgres 16 container directus-db-nzli85mk3luzb6u7cnq5fidu
547	```
548	
549	Use custom-format dump where possible so restore can be inspected and replayed
550	selectively.
551	
552	### 7.2 Raw/Staging Load
553	
554	Load current Directus rows into staging/import structures before merging.
555	
556	Preserve:
557	
558	- Directus UUIDs
559	- `external_id`
560	- `external_source`
561	- source table name
562	- raw payload where useful
563	- timestamps
564	
565	Use:
566	
567	```text
568	ingest.sync_run
569	ingest.raw_record
570	core.company_source_ref
571	core.contact_source_ref
572	core.factory_source_ref
573	core.taxonomy_source_ref
574	core.sku_ref
575	```
576	
577	### 7.3 Canonical Company/Contact Build
578	
579	Companies:
580	
581	- Directus `retailer`
582	- Directus `ingested_domains`
583	- PLM customers
584	- DAM customer/path/PO customer references
585	
586	must resolve into:
587	
588	```text
589	core.company
590	core.company_source_ref
591	```
592	
593	Contacts:
594	
595	- Directus `buyer`
596	- Directus `ingested_contact`
597	- future PLM contact/vendor data
598	
599	must resolve into:
600	
601	```text
602	core.contact
603	core.contact_company
604	core.contact_source_ref
605	```
606	
607	Do not hard-merge uncertain duplicates. Use:
608	
609	```text
610	ingest.dedupe_candidate
611	```
612	
613	for ambiguous matches.
614	
615	### 7.4 CRM-Owned Table Load
616	
617	Load CRM-owned data after canonical `core` rows exist:
618	
619	```text
620	crm.department
621	crm.opportunity
622	crm.opportunity_product
623	crm.email_message
624	crm.meeting_note
625	crm.ignore_rule
626	crm.ai_model_config
627	crm.note
628	crm.task
629	crm.licensor_approval_thread
630	```
631	
632	Relationship mapping examples:
633	
634	| Current Directus field | Target FK |
635	|---|---|
636	| `crm_department.retailer` | `crm.department.company_id` |
637	| `crm_department.primary_buyer` | `crm.department.primary_contact_id` |
638	| `crm_opportunity.retailer` | `crm.opportunity.company_id` |
639	| `crm_opportunity.contact` | `crm.opportunity.contact_id` |
640	| `crm_opportunity.department` | `crm.opportunity.department_id` |
641	| `crm_opportunity.factory` | `crm.opportunity.factory_id` |
642	| `crm_opportunity.project` | `crm.opportunity.project_id` |
643	| `crm_email_message.retailer` | `crm.email_message.company_id` |
644	| `crm_email_message.opportunity` | `crm.email_message.opportunity_id` |
645	| `crm_meeting_note.retailer` | `crm.meeting_note.company_id` |
646	| `crm_task.assignee` | `crm.task.assignee_profile_id` |
647	
648	### 7.5 Data Reconciliation
649	
650	Run reconciliation after each rehearsal:
651	
652	- row count by table
653	- orphan FK checks
654	- account count by customer status
655	- contact count by account/customer status
656	- department count by company
657	- opportunity count by stage
658	- email count by routing status
659	- latest email received timestamp
660	- meeting count and latest meeting timestamp
661	- task count by status
662	- approvals count
663	- null/free-form approval status behavior
664	- source-ref coverage for all imported rows
665	
666	Do not proceed to frontend cutover until reconciliation is documented.

731	export const SUPABASE_ANON_KEY = import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

738	  auth: {
739	    persistSession: true,
740	    autoRefreshToken: true,
741	    detectSessionInUrl: true,
742	  },

765	- `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'azure', options: { redirectTo } })`

793	
794	Rewrite `src/features/crm/api.ts` function by function. Keep exported function
795	names stable at first so pages/components do not all change at once.
796	
797	Suggested migration order:
798	
799	1. Auth/profile and app access.
800	2. `fetchRetailers` / `fetchIngestedDomains` / `updateRetailer`.
801	3. `fetchBuyers` / `fetchIngestedContacts` / `updateBuyer`.
802	4. `fetchDepartments`, `createDepartment`, `updateDepartment`, `deleteDepartment`.
803	5. `fetchOpportunities`, `setOpportunityStage`, `updateOpportunity`.

809	11. `fetchTasks`, `createTask`, `updateTask`.

811	13. `askOpportunityAi` worker endpoint compatibility.

813	Use `api` views for reads where useful. Use base tables or RPCs for writes.
814	
815	Example mapping:
816	
817	```ts
818	// Directus
819	readItems('crm_opportunity', {
820	  fields: ['id', 'name', { retailer: ['id', 'name'] }],
821	})
822	
823	// Supabase view/table approach
824	supabase
825	  .from('crm_opportunity_list') // if exposed under api and generated types allow it
826	  .select('*')
827	
828	// Or schema-qualified direct table if needed
829	supabase
830	  .schema('crm')
831	  .from('opportunity')
832	  .select(`
833	    id,
834	    name,
835	    company:company_id(id, name)
836	  `)
837	```
838	
839	Use the actual generated type and Supabase query syntax supported by the current
840	client version.

842	### 8.5 Preserve Loader Resilience
843	
844	`CrmDataContext.load()` currently uses `Promise.allSettled`. This is intentional.
845	
846	Do not regress to all-or-nothing loading.
847	
848	Reason:
849	
850	One bad collection/RLS issue should not blank every CRM page. A prior Directus
851	schema mismatch blanked the whole app when the loader used all-or-nothing logic.
852	
853	Keep per-resource failure handling and useful diagnostics.
854	
855	### 8.6 Types
856	
857	Replace hand-maintained Directus-shaped types gradually.
858	
859	Recommended pattern:
860	
861	- Keep current UI/domain types temporarily.
862	- Add adapter functions from Supabase rows/views to UI types.
863	- Use generated `Database` types for Supabase query outputs.
864	- Avoid broad `any`.
865	- Do not rename all UI-facing types in the same commit as the backend switch unless necessary.

932	## Phase 10: Department/Company Integrity
933	
934	Current Directus/Postgres SQL enforces that selected departments belong to the
935	selected account/company.
936	
937	Equivalent constraints must exist in shared-db.
938	
939	Rules:
940	
941	- `crm.department.company_id` is required.
942	- Department names should be unique per company.
943	- `crm.opportunity.department_id`, if set, must belong to `crm.opportunity.company_id`.
944	- `crm.email_message.department_id`, if introduced, must belong to its company.
945	- `crm.meeting_note.department_id`, if introduced, must belong to its company.
946	- `crm.note.department_id`, if introduced, must belong to its company.
947	- `crm.task.department_id`, if introduced, must belong to its company.
948	
949	If these fields are in metadata or omitted in baseline, add explicit columns and
950	triggers before the frontend allows editing them.
951	
952	Reference current SQL:
953	
954	```text
955	/worksp/directus/pm-system/sql/enforce-crm-department-scope.sql
956	```

1110	
1111	```text
1112	administrator
1113	sales
1114	licensing
1115	viewer
1116	vendor
1117	```
1118	
1119	Verify:
1120	
1121	- administrator can read/write expected CRM data
1122	- sales can read/write expected CRM data
1123	- licensing can read/write expected CRM/licensing approval data
1124	- viewer can read but not write
1125	- vendor cannot read CRM unless explicitly scoped
1126	- raw ingest is not visible
1127	- sensitive email/meeting raw payloads are not visible through broad views
1128	- PLM cost/RFQ data is not visible through CRM views
1129	
1130	### 14.5 Worker Tests
1131	
1132	Against preview:
1133	
1134	- `/health` returns 200
1135	- Fireflies webhook can create or skip duplicate meeting note correctly
1136	- opportunity chat endpoint answers against Supabase data
1137	- Outlook ingest inserts `crm.email_message`
1138	- reroute updates routing status and links
1139	- contact sync creates/links canonical company/contact/source refs as designed
1140	- apply ignore rules updates email status and counters
1141	- summarize updates opportunity AI summary fields if preserved
1142	
1143	## Phase 15: Production Promotion
1144	
1145	Production promotion is migration-file based.
1146	
1147	In `u2giants/shared-db`:
1148	
1149	```bash
1150	supabase link --project-ref qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai
1151	scripts/check-sql.sh
1152	supabase db push --dry-run
1153	```
1154	
1155	Review the dry run carefully.
1156	
1157	For the first production promotion, production may not yet have the baseline
1158	shared schema migrations. Expect the baseline migrations plus approved CRM
1159	migrations unless rollout is deliberately split.
1160	
1161	Do not proceed if dry-run includes unapproved migrations.
1162	
1163	During approved production window:
1164	
1165	```bash
1166	supabase db push
1167	supabase migration list
1168	```
1169	
1170	Then update production CRM env:
1171	
1172	```text
1173	VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co
1174	VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<production anon key>
1175	VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN=<existing optional value>
1176	```
1177	
1178	Switch CRM worker env:
1179	
1180	```text
1181	SUPABASE_URL=https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co
1182	SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=<production service-role key>
1183	```
1184	
1185	## Phase 16: Cutover Order
1186	
1187	Use this exact order unless the owner approves a different sequence.
1188	
1189	1. Confirm preview branch tests are complete.
1190	2. Confirm shared-db migrations are committed and reviewed.
1191	3. Schedule a CRM write freeze.
1192	4. Freeze Directus CRM writes.
1193	5. Take fresh Directus Postgres dump.
1194	6. Run final data import/delta into shared Supabase production.
1195	7. Run reconciliation reports.
1196	8. Apply production shared-db migrations if not already applied.
1197	9. Deploy Supabase-backed CRM frontend.
1198	10. Switch CRM worker to production Supabase.
1199	11. Smoke test production login and all CRM routes.
1200	12. Smoke test worker endpoints.
1201	13. Monitor logs/RLS errors/query failures.
1202	14. Keep Directus available read-only for rollback/reference.
1203	
1204	## Phase 17: Rollback Plan
1205	
1206	Rollback is Directus-first.
1207	
1208	If production cutover fails:
1209	
1210	1. Redeploy the last known Directus-backed `popcrm-web` image.
1211	2. Restore frontend env to Directus configuration.
1212	3. Point CRM worker back to Directus.
1213	4. Re-enable Directus writes.
1214	5. Keep Supabase writes for inspection; do not replay them blindly.
1215	
1216	Avoid browser dual-writes during migration. Dual-writes make rollback and data
1217	trust much harder.
1218	
1219	## Phase 18: Required Handoff
1220	
1221	Each migration session must leave a handoff note in the canonical shared-db repo:
1222	
1223	```text
1224	docs/app-migration-notes/popcrm-web-YYYYMMDD.md
1225	```
1226	
1227	Include:
1228	
1229	- Supabase tables used by each CRM screen.
1230	- API views used by each CRM screen.
1231	- RPCs used by each CRM action.
1232	- New migrations added.
1233	- RLS policies changed.
1234	- Realtime subscriptions used.
1235	- Data import scripts run.
1236	- Reconciliation results.
1237	- Preview branch test results.
1238	- Production promotion checklist.
1239	- Exact production migrations to apply.
1240	- Known gaps.
1241	- Screens still using Directus, if any.
1242	- Worker functionality still using Directus, if any.
1243	
1244	Also update this CRM repo docs if frontend env/config behavior changes.
1245	
1246	## Implementation Checklist
1247	
1248	Use this as the working checklist.
1249	
1250	### Shared-DB Work
1251	
1252	- [ ] Clone/open `u2giants/shared-db`.
1253	- [ ] Read required shared-db docs.
1254	- [ ] Link Supabase CLI to preview `tcscehehgeiijilylezv`.
1255	- [ ] Audit current Directus CRM fields and worker fields.
1256	- [ ] Compare Directus field usage to baseline shared schema.
1257	- [ ] Add missing CRM parity fields where justified.
1258	- [ ] Add CRM API views/RPCs.
1259	- [ ] Add indexes for CRM query paths.
1260	- [ ] Add department/company integrity triggers if needed.
1261	- [ ] Tighten CRM RLS policies.
1262	- [ ] Update realtime publication only where appropriate.
1263	- [ ] Run `scripts/check-sql.sh`.
1264	- [ ] Run `supabase db push --dry-run`.
1265	- [ ] Apply migrations to preview only.
1266	- [ ] Commit migrations and docs to shared-db.
1267	
1268	### Data Migration Work
1269	
1270	- [ ] Take fresh Directus dump for rehearsal.
1271	- [ ] Load raw/staging rows into preview.
1272	- [ ] Populate `core.*_source_ref`.
1273	- [ ] Build canonical companies.
1274	- [ ] Build canonical contacts and contact-company relations.
1275	- [ ] Build canonical factories.
1276	- [ ] Load CRM-owned tables.
1277	- [ ] Run row-count reconciliation.
1278	- [ ] Run FK orphan checks.
1279	- [ ] Run source-ref coverage checks.
1280	- [ ] Document uncertain dedupe candidates.
1281	
1282	### CRM Frontend Work
1283	
1284	- [ ] Remove `@directus/sdk`.
1285	- [ ] Install `@supabase/supabase-js`.
1286	- [ ] Add `src/lib/supabase.ts`.
1287	- [ ] Generate `src/lib/database.types.ts` from preview.
1288	- [ ] Rewrite auth provider.
1289	- [ ] Rewrite login page OAuth/password flows.
1290	- [ ] Rewrite `src/features/crm/api.ts` read functions.
1291	- [ ] Rewrite `src/features/crm/api.ts` write functions.
1292	- [ ] Preserve `CrmDataContext` `Promise.allSettled` resilience.
1293	- [ ] Update types/adapters.
1294	- [ ] Update docs/configuration env vars.
1295	- [ ] Run lint/build.
1296	
1297	### CRM Worker Work
1298	
1299	- [ ] Port worker auth from Directus login to Supabase service role.
1300	- [ ] Port Outlook ingest.
1301	- [ ] Port reroute.
1302	- [ ] Port Fireflies webhook.
1303	- [ ] Port contact sync.
1304	- [ ] Port opportunity summaries.
1305	- [ ] Port ignore-rule application.
1306	- [ ] Port opportunity chat.
1307	- [ ] Test worker against preview.
1308	- [ ] Keep service-role key server-only.
1309	
1310	### Preview Test Work
1311	
1312	- [ ] Deploy Supabase-backed build to `crm-dev.designflow.app`.
1313	- [ ] Test Microsoft login.
1314	- [ ] Test every CRM page.
1315	- [ ] Test create/update actions.
1316	- [ ] Test role-specific RLS.
1317	- [ ] Test worker endpoints.
1318	- [ ] Document preview test results in shared-db handoff.
1319	
1320	### Production Cutover Work
1321	
1322	- [ ] Review production `supabase db push --dry-run`.
1323	- [ ] Approve production migration list.
1324	- [ ] Freeze Directus writes.
1325	- [ ] Run final import/delta.
1326	- [ ] Run final reconciliation.
1327	- [ ] Apply production migrations.
1328	- [ ] Deploy Supabase-backed CRM production.
1329	- [ ] Switch worker to production Supabase.
1330	- [ ] Smoke test production.
1331	- [ ] Keep Directus read-only for rollback.
1332	
1333	## Common Mistakes To Avoid
1334	
1335	- Do not target `bhnrhaqesgomgeuppbjp`; use shared-db target refs unless docs are updated.
1336	- Do not create a CRM-specific Supabase project.
1337	- Do not put permanent DDL in `popcrm-web`.
1338	- Do not edit mirrored `shared-db/` copies in consumer repos.
1339	- Do not run production SQL manually in Supabase dashboard.
1340	- Do not expose `ingest.raw_record` to browser clients.
1341	- Do not expose raw email bodies broadly.
1342	- Do not expose PLM/RFQ/cost base tables through CRM views.
1343	- Do not move DigitalOcean Spaces storage during this migration.
1344	- Do not reintroduce all-or-nothing CRM bootstrap loading.
1345	- Do not trust frontend role checks as authorization.
1346	- Do not grant vendor CRM/product/order access before scoped rules exist.
1347	- Do not dual-write from browser code.
1348	- Do not hard-merge uncertain company/contact/factory/taxonomy duplicates.
1349	
1350	## Final Definition Of Done
1351	
1352	The CRM backend migration is done only when:
1353	
1354	- Shared-db migrations are committed and applied to production through Supabase CLI.
1355	- CRM data is imported into shared schemas with reconciliation documented.
1356	- CRM frontend no longer imports or uses `@directus/sdk`.
1357	- CRM auth uses Supabase Auth and `app.profile`.
1358	- CRM reads use shared `core`, `crm`, `pim`, and `api` contracts.
1359	- CRM writes are protected by RLS/RPCs.
1360	- CRM worker writes to Supabase with service-role credentials.
1361	- All CRM pages pass preview and production smoke tests.
1362	- Role-specific RLS tests pass.
1363	- Directus remains only as read-only rollback/reference during the agreed stabilization period.
1364	
1365

# CRM Backend Migration Plan: Directus To Shared Supabase

This document is the execution plan for migrating `u2giants/popcrm-web` from the
current Directus backend to the shared enterprise-wide Supabase backend.

It is written for a fresh AI/developer session with no prior chat context. Read
this file completely before making changes.

## Executive Summary

The CRM app currently reads and writes directly to a shared Directus API at
`https://data.designflow.app`. The new strategy is not to create a CRM-specific
Supabase project. Instead, CRM, PM/PIM, DAM, and operational PLM data will live
in one shared Supabase.com project with logical schemas.

The CRM migration has two parts:

1. Backend/data-layer work in the canonical shared database repo.
2. Frontend and worker rewrites in this CRM repo and the existing CRM worker.

Do not create a separate Supabase project for CRM.

Do not put permanent database DDL in this repo.

Do not run unreviewed SQL directly against production.

## Canonical Shared-DB References

The shared database plan is maintained in `u2giants/shared-db` and mirrored into
consumer repos under `shared-db/`. Mirrored copies are read-only and overwritten
by sync.

If the local checkout does not contain `shared-db/`, use the GitHub links below.

Primary shared-db docs:

- [Shared DB README](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/tree/main/shared-db)
- [Shared database vision](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/shared-database-vision.md)
- [Unified Supabase schema map](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md)
- [Unified Supabase relationships](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-relationships.md)
- [Unified Supabase migration gaps](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/unified-supabase-migration-gaps.md)
- [Supabase migration preparation](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/supabase-migration-prep.md)
- [Schema implementation notes](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/implementation/schema-implementation-notes.md)
- [Preview branch verification](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/verification/preview-branch-20260621.md)
- [Shared Supabase branch workflow](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/ai-session-instructions/shared-supabase-branch-workflow.md)
- [CRM-specific Supabase migration guide](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/ai-session-instructions/popcrm-web-supabase-migration.md)
- [AI session instructions index](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/docs/ai-session-instructions/README.md)

Baseline SQL migrations in shared-db:

- [20260621000100_foundation.sql](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260621000100_foundation.sql)
- [20260621000200_app_core.sql](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260621000200_app_core.sql)
- [20260621000300_domain_tables.sql](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260621000300_domain_tables.sql)
- [20260621000400_api_rls_realtime.sql](https://github.com/u2giants/popcrm-web/blob/main/shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260621000400_api_rls_realtime.sql)

## Supabase Targets

Use the shared Supabase project refs from the shared-db docs.

Preview branch:

```text
Project ref: tcscehehgeiijilylezv
URL: https://tcscehehgeiijilylezv.supabase.co
Branch name: shared-db-schema-rehearsal
Purpose: shared integration target for CRM/PM rewrite work
```

Production/default project:

```text
Project ref: qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai
URL: https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co
Purpose: live shared enterprise Supabase project; currently live PopDAM base
```

Important:

- Apply new CRM database migrations to preview first.
- Production promotion must happen through committed shared-db migration files.
- Do not manually copy SQL objects from preview to production in the Supabase dashboard.
- The previously discussed project ref `bhnrhaqesgomgeuppbjp` is not the active target for this shared strategy unless the owner explicitly changes the shared-db docs.

## Current CRM Architecture

`popcrm-web` is a Vite/React/TypeScript SPA. It stores no local business data.
All current business reads/writes go through the shared Directus backend at
`https://data.designflow.app`.

Production app:

```text
https://crm.designflow.app
```

Preview alias:

```text
https://crm-dev.designflow.app
```

Current Directus backend:

```text
https://data.designflow.app
```

Current Fireflies/CRM worker endpoint:

```text
https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app
```

## Current Directus Dependency Surface In This Repo

The CRM frontend is fairly well centralized around a small number of Directus
entry points. Migrate these deliberately.

Files to rewrite or adapt:

| File | Current role |
|---|---|
| `src/lib/directus.ts` | Directus SDK client, Directus URL, session-cookie auth mode, Microsoft SSO URL helper, Directus asset URL helper. |
| `src/auth/auth.tsx` | Directus `readMe`, login, logout, current user state. |
| `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` | Calls `microsoftLoginUrl()` and email/password login. |
| `src/features/crm/api.ts` | All CRM collection reads/writes using `@directus/sdk`. |
| `src/features/crm/CrmDataContext.tsx` | Bootstrap loader. Must preserve `Promise.allSettled` behavior. |
| `src/lib/types.ts` | Hand-maintained Directus-shaped schema slice. |
| `src/features/crm/constants.ts` | Enum/status logic; may need updates if Supabase values differ. |

Search command to verify Directus usage:

```bash
rg "directus|@directus/sdk|readItems|createItem|updateItem|deleteItem|readMe|microsoftLoginUrl" src -n
```

Expected current direct dependencies include:

- `src/lib/directus.ts`
- `src/auth/auth.tsx`
- `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx`
- `src/features/crm/api.ts`
- occasional constant/docs references

## Current Directus Backend/Worker Sources

The CRM backend schema and worker currently live in the sibling Directus repo.
Use these as source references while designing migrations and worker behavior.

Relevant files:

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-schema.mjs` | Directus CRM schema source: collections, fields, relations, enums. |
| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-roles.mjs` | Current Directus role grants for CRM collections. |
| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-worker.mjs` | Outlook ingest, reroute, Fireflies webhook, contact sync, summaries, ignore-rule application, opportunity chat. |
| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/sql/enforce-crm-department-scope.sql` | SQL trigger logic enforcing department belongs to selected customer. |
| `/worksp/directus/pm-system/migration/split-customers-from-ingested.sql` | Current split between curated customer tables and raw ingested registries. |

If `/worksp/directus` is unavailable in a fresh environment, clone or inspect the
`u2giants/directus` repo before writing backend migrations.

## Current CRM Collections And Shared Supabase Targets

Do not carry Directus collection names forward blindly. The shared database uses
canonical schemas.

| Current Directus collection | Shared Supabase target | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `retailer` | `core.company` | Curated customers/accounts. One canonical company table shared by CRM, PM, DAM, and PLM. |
| `ingested_domains` | `core.company_source_ref` and/or `ingest.raw_record` | Raw ingested company/domain registry. Do not expose directly to broad browser contracts. |
| `buyer` | `core.contact`, `core.contact_company` | Curated contacts/buyers. Contact-company relation carries account relationship. |
| `ingested_contact` | `core.contact_source_ref` and/or `ingest.raw_record` | Raw ingested contact registry. Do not expose directly to broad browser contracts. |
| `factory` | `core.factory` | Canonical factory/vendor identity shared with PM and PLM. |
| `project` | `pim.project` | PM/PIM project. CRM opportunity may link to it. |
| `crm_department` | `crm.department` | CRM-owned account department segmentation, linked to `core.company`. |
| `crm_opportunity` | `crm.opportunity` | CRM-owned sales/program pipeline, linked to `core.company`, `core.contact`, `crm.department`, `pim.project`, and possibly PLM production orders. |
| `crm_email_message` | `crm.email_message` | CRM-owned communication/routing record. Raw/sensitive bodies must not be broadly exposed. |
| `crm_meeting_note` | `crm.meeting_note` | CRM-owned meeting notes and Fireflies-derived summaries. |
| `crm_ignore_rule` | `crm.ignore_rule` | CRM-owned routing skip rules. |
| `crm_ai_model_config` | `crm.ai_model_config` | CRM-owned model settings. |
| `crm_note` | `crm.note` or later `app.comment` | Keep in `crm.note` initially unless the note becomes cross-domain. |
| `crm_task` | `crm.task` | CRM-owned task workflow. |
| `crm_licensor_approval_thread` | `crm.licensor_approval_thread` | CRM approval workflow; can link to PM submissions/revisions where known. |
| `directus_users` | `app.profile` | Supabase Auth owns identity; app profile owns business profile data. |

Hard rule:

Do not create duplicate `crm.company`, `crm.contact`, `crm.project`,
`crm.factory`, or CRM-only taxonomy tables. Shared identity/reference objects
belong in `core` or `pim`.

## Existing Baseline Shared Schema

The shared-db baseline migrations already implement these logical schemas:

```text
app
core
dam
pim
crm
plm
ingest
api
```

Important baseline objects:

```text
app.profile
app.role
app.user_role
app.app_access
app.comment
app.activity
app.notification
app.file_object

core.company
core.company_source_ref
core.contact
core.contact_source_ref
core.contact_company
core.licensor
core.property
core.character
core.factory
core.factory_source_ref
core.product_category
core.product_type
core.product_subtype
core.sku_ref

crm.department
crm.opportunity
crm.opportunity_product
crm.email_message
crm.meeting_note
crm.note
crm.task
crm.ignore_rule
crm.ai_model_config
crm.licensor_approval_thread

pim.project
pim.product
pim.product_submission
pim.revision_request

ingest.sync_run
ingest.raw_record
ingest.dedupe_candidate

api.crm_account_overview
api.global_search
```

The baseline is intentionally first-pass. It does not necessarily cover every
field or screen contract needed by the current CRM frontend.

## High-Level Migration Phases

Do the work in this order:

1. Read shared-db docs and current CRM code.
2. Audit current Directus collection fields and CRM frontend field usage.
3. Add missing shared-db migrations on the preview branch.
4. Build API views/RPCs for CRM screen contracts.
5. Design/import data from Directus into shared schemas.
6. Rewrite CRM frontend auth and API access to Supabase.
7. Port the CRM worker to Supabase service-role access.
8. Test everything against the preview branch.
9. Promote migrations to production through shared-db.
10. Cut over CRM frontend and worker.
11. Keep Directus read-only for rollback/reference during the stabilization period.

## Phase 1: Read And Audit

Start by reading the shared-db docs listed above. Then inspect the CRM frontend.

Commands:

```bash
pwd
rg "directus|@directus/sdk|readItems|createItem|updateItem|deleteItem|readMe|microsoftLoginUrl" src -n
sed -n '1,260p' src/features/crm/api.ts
sed -n '1,260p' src/lib/types.ts
sed -n '1,220p' src/auth/auth.tsx
sed -n '1,220p' src/lib/directus.ts
sed -n '1,260p' src/features/crm/CrmDataContext.tsx
```

If available, inspect the Directus backend source:

```bash
sed -n '1,460p' /worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-schema.mjs
sed -n '1,260p' /worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-roles.mjs
sed -n '1,1120p' /worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-worker.mjs
sed -n '1,180p' /worksp/directus/pm-system/sql/enforce-crm-department-scope.sql
sed -n '1,220p' /worksp/directus/pm-system/migration/split-customers-from-ingested.sql
```

Deliverable from this phase:

- A field-level inventory of every Directus field the CRM frontend or worker reads/writes.
- A gap list comparing those fields to existing shared-db baseline tables.
- A list of screens that can use existing `api` views and screens requiring new views/RPCs.

## Phase 2: Shared-DB Migration Workflow

All database changes go in `u2giants/shared-db`, not this repo.

From a checkout of `u2giants/shared-db`:

```bash
supabase link --project-ref tcscehehgeiijilylezv
scripts/check-sql.sh
supabase db push --dry-run
```

Create new timestamped migrations for CRM-specific work:

```text
supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_crm_api_contracts.sql
supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_crm_directus_parity_fields.sql
supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_crm_worker_rpc.sql
supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_crm_rls_tightening.sql
supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_crm_realtime_contracts.sql
```

Naming rules:

- Use unique timestamps.
- Include `crm_` in CRM-specific migration names.
- Keep changes additive where possible.
- Add comments for security-sensitive policies and RPCs.

Apply only to preview first:

```bash
scripts/check-sql.sh
supabase db push --dry-run
supabase db push
supabase migration list
```

Never use production project ref while developing.

## Phase 3: Shared Schema Gap Resolution

Compare current CRM needs to baseline tables.

Known likely gaps:

| Current CRM need | Baseline status | Action |
|---|---|---|
| Account status values like `ACTIVE_CUSTOMER`, `POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER`, `OTHER`, `UNASSIGNED` | `core.company.status` is generic `app.entity_status`; app-specific status may need metadata or a dedicated field. | Decide whether to add `core.company.customer_status` or store Directus CRM status in `metadata`. Prefer explicit field if it drives UI and filters. |
| Retailer domain/routing aliases | `core.company.domain` exists; routing aliases likely not first-class. | Add explicit field or metadata contract if email routing needs it. |
| Buyer/contact type and scope | `core.contact.title` and `core.contact_company.relationship_type` exist; CRM-specific `contact_type`/`scope` may be missing. | Add fields to `core.contact_company` or `crm` bridge table; do not duplicate contact. |
| CRM department category/division/active | `crm.department` baseline has `status` and metadata only. | Add explicit fields if UI filters/edits them. |
| Opportunity fields `amount`, `program_type`, `season_year`, `division`, PO/SO/hard delivery, AI fields | Baseline has `estimated_value`, `production_po_number`, `sales_order_number`, metadata. | Add explicit columns for fields used broadly; keep rare/import fields in metadata. |
| Email routing fields `routing_status`, `routing_method`, `sender`, `recipients`, detected numbers | Baseline has `status`, `direction`, `subject`, `body_preview`, `body_storage_ref`, metadata. | Add explicit routing columns or create API view that maps metadata to expected shape. |
| Meeting `participants`, `action_items`, `source`, `fireflies_transcript_id` | Baseline has `title`, `body`, `meeting_at`, external refs, metadata. | Add explicit fields or define metadata contract. |
| Notes `title`, `action_items`, source fields | Baseline `crm.note` has `body` only. | Add columns if current UI depends on them. |
| Task `body`, department/contact/opportunity relations | Baseline task has company/opportunity/assignee/title/status/due. | Add missing relations/fields. |
| Approval free-form fields `property_name`, `stage`, dates, comments | Baseline approval thread has subject/status and links. | Add free-form stage and current date/comment columns; do not convert to enum. |

Important principle:

Do not force all old Directus fields into 1:1 columns. Use explicit columns for
fields that are queried, filtered, displayed prominently, or edited. Use
`metadata` for low-value historical/import fields.

## Phase 4: API Views And RPC Contracts

The CRM frontend should not manually stitch many raw tables for every screen.
Use browser-facing `api` views/RPCs for joined, RLS-safe contracts.

Existing baseline views:

```text
api.crm_account_overview
api.global_search
```

Add new CRM contracts as needed:

| CRM screen | Recommended contract |
|---|---|
| Overview | `api.crm_account_overview`, plus `api.crm_activity_summary` or specific rollup RPCs. |
| Accounts | `api.crm_account_list` |
| Contacts | `api.crm_contact_list` |
| Departments | `api.crm_department_contacts` |
| Pipeline | `api.crm_opportunity_board`, `api.crm_opportunity_list` |
| Programs | `api.crm_opportunity_list` |
| Email Routing | `api.crm_email_routing_queue` |
| Meetings | `api.crm_meeting_list` |
| Notes | `api.crm_note_list` |
| Tasks | `api.crm_task_list` |
| Approvals | `api.crm_approval_queue` |
| Settings | direct `crm.ai_model_config` or `api.crm_settings_models` |
| Command search | `api.global_search`, possibly extended |

View design requirements:

- Use `security_invoker = true`.
- Select only fields intended for browser use.
- Do not expose raw email bodies or broad raw meeting payloads.
- Do not expose `ingest.raw_record`.
- Do not expose PLM/RFQ/cost base tables.
- Include stable aliases that are convenient for frontend code.
- Keep write behavior on base tables or through RPCs, not writeable views unless deliberately designed.

RPCs are appropriate for workflow actions that require multiple writes or
security checks:

```text
api.crm_apply_email_route(...)
api.crm_create_ignore_rule_from_email(...)
api.crm_update_opportunity_stage(...)
api.crm_create_note_for_opportunity(...)
api.crm_assign_task(...)
```

If a CRM action should update PM data immediately, use a database trigger or
service-side RPC/function. Do not dual-write from browser code.

## Phase 5: Identity, Auth, App Access, And Roles

Directus users do not transfer directly.

Target identity model:

```text
auth.users
app.profile
app.role
app.user_role
app.app_access
```

Supabase Auth owns authentication. `app.profile` owns app-facing profile data.
`app.user_role` and `app.app_access` drive RLS helper functions.

Expected roles:

```text
administrator
sales
licensing
designer
viewer
vendor
```

CRM access should require either:

```sql
app.has_app_access('crm')
```

or administrator role:

```sql
app.has_role('administrator')
```

Initial behavior:

| Role | CRM access |
|---|---|
| `administrator` | Full access. |
| `sales` | CRM read/write for operational CRM tables. |
| `licensing` | CRM read/write where licensing/approval work requires it. |
| `designer` | No broad CRM writes unless explicitly approved. Read access only if business needs it. |
| `viewer` | Read-only if granted CRM app access. |
| `vendor` | No CRM access unless a future scoped requirement is designed. |

Implementation tasks:

1. Configure Microsoft/Azure provider in Supabase Auth.
2. Import/map Directus users into `app.profile`.
3. Map Directus/Entra roles into `app.role` / `app.user_role`.
4. Grant `app.app_access` rows for CRM users.
5. Decide how Entra group membership becomes Supabase role state.
6. Do not trust browser-provided role values for authorization.

If Entra remains the role hub, use a server-side sync or custom access-token hook
strategy. Do not rely on client-side role claims without RLS verification.

## Phase 6: RLS Requirements

The baseline shared-db RLS is a scaffold. Tighten and test before production.

CRM RLS requirements:

- Authenticated CRM users can read only intended CRM data.
- Sales/licensing can write only intended CRM records.
- Viewers cannot write.
- Vendors cannot read CRM data unless explicitly scoped later.
- `ingest.*` is admin/service-only.
- `core.*_source_ref` tables are admin/service-only by default.
- Raw email/meeting payloads are not exposed through broad views.
- Worker/admin RPCs require service role or explicit admin checks.
- PLM/RFQ/cost/pricing tables are not exposed to CRM users through broad grants.

Test with real Supabase users for each role:

```text
administrator
sales
licensing
viewer
vendor
```

Do not mark migration complete until role-specific reads/writes have been tested
against the preview branch.

## Phase 7: Data Migration Design

Use Directus/Postgres data as the source. Do not rely on Directus API exports as
the primary migration path.

Recommended import order:

1. Take a fresh Directus Postgres dump.
2. Load source rows into staging or `ingest.raw_record`.
3. Create source-reference rows.
4. Build canonical `core` rows.
5. Load CRM-owned `crm` rows with FKs to canonical rows.
6. Verify row counts and relationships.
7. Only then point frontend preview at the data.

Detailed sequence:

### 7.1 Source Dump

Take a fresh source dump before each rehearsal.

Source Directus database context from existing docs:

```text
Production API/Data Studio: https://data.designflow.app
Source database: Postgres 16 container directus-db-nzli85mk3luzb6u7cnq5fidu
```

Use custom-format dump where possible so restore can be inspected and replayed
selectively.

### 7.2 Raw/Staging Load

Load current Directus rows into staging/import structures before merging.

Preserve:

- Directus UUIDs
- `external_id`
- `external_source`
- source table name
- raw payload where useful
- timestamps

Use:

```text
ingest.sync_run
ingest.raw_record
core.company_source_ref
core.contact_source_ref
core.factory_source_ref
core.taxonomy_source_ref
core.sku_ref
```

### 7.3 Canonical Company/Contact Build

Companies:

- Directus `retailer`
- Directus `ingested_domains`
- PLM customers
- DAM customer/path/PO customer references

must resolve into:

```text
core.company
core.company_source_ref
```

Contacts:

- Directus `buyer`
- Directus `ingested_contact`
- future PLM contact/vendor data

must resolve into:

```text
core.contact
core.contact_company
core.contact_source_ref
```

Do not hard-merge uncertain duplicates. Use:

```text
ingest.dedupe_candidate
```

for ambiguous matches.

### 7.4 CRM-Owned Table Load

Load CRM-owned data after canonical `core` rows exist:

```text
crm.department
crm.opportunity
crm.opportunity_product
crm.email_message
crm.meeting_note
crm.ignore_rule
crm.ai_model_config
crm.note
crm.task
crm.licensor_approval_thread
```

Relationship mapping examples:

| Current Directus field | Target FK |
|---|---|
| `crm_department.retailer` | `crm.department.company_id` |
| `crm_department.primary_buyer` | `crm.department.primary_contact_id` |
| `crm_opportunity.retailer` | `crm.opportunity.company_id` |
| `crm_opportunity.contact` | `crm.opportunity.contact_id` |
| `crm_opportunity.department` | `crm.opportunity.department_id` |
| `crm_opportunity.factory` | `crm.opportunity.factory_id` |
| `crm_opportunity.project` | `crm.opportunity.project_id` |
| `crm_email_message.retailer` | `crm.email_message.company_id` |
| `crm_email_message.opportunity` | `crm.email_message.opportunity_id` |
| `crm_meeting_note.retailer` | `crm.meeting_note.company_id` |
| `crm_task.assignee` | `crm.task.assignee_profile_id` |

### 7.5 Data Reconciliation

Run reconciliation after each rehearsal:

- row count by table
- orphan FK checks
- account count by customer status
- contact count by account/customer status
- department count by company
- opportunity count by stage
- email count by routing status
- latest email received timestamp
- meeting count and latest meeting timestamp
- task count by status
- approvals count
- null/free-form approval status behavior
- source-ref coverage for all imported rows

Do not proceed to frontend cutover until reconciliation is documented.

## Phase 8: Frontend Supabase Rewrite

Install Supabase client:

```bash
npm remove @directus/sdk
npm install @supabase/supabase-js
```

Add:

```text
src/lib/supabase.ts
src/lib/database.types.ts
```

Generate types from preview:

```bash
supabase gen types typescript --project-id tcscehehgeiijilylezv > src/lib/database.types.ts
```

Do not commit `.env` with keys.

Preview frontend env:

```text
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://tcscehehgeiijilylezv.supabase.co
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<preview anon key from approved secret store>
VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN=<unchanged optional existing value>
```

Production frontend env after cutover:

```text
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<production anon key from approved secret store>
VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN=<unchanged optional existing value>
```

### 8.1 Replace `src/lib/directus.ts`

Current Directus module provides:

- `DIRECTUS_URL`
- `directus`
- `microsoftLoginUrl`
- `assetUrl`

New Supabase module should provide:

- `SUPABASE_URL`
- `supabase`
- optional `signInWithMicrosoft()` helper
- no Directus asset helper unless a temporary compatibility path is needed

Example shape:

```ts
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
import type { Database } from './database.types'

export const SUPABASE_URL = import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL
export const SUPABASE_ANON_KEY = import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

if (!SUPABASE_URL || !SUPABASE_ANON_KEY) {
  throw new Error('Missing Supabase environment configuration')
}

export const supabase = createClient<Database>(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, {
  auth: {
    persistSession: true,
    autoRefreshToken: true,
    detectSessionInUrl: true,
  },
})
```

Exact implementation should match current app patterns and lint/build rules.

### 8.2 Rewrite Auth

Rewrite `src/auth/auth.tsx`.

Current Directus behavior:

- `readMe()`
- `directus.login(email, password)`
- `directus.logout()`
- session cookie mode

Target Supabase behavior:

- `supabase.auth.getSession()`
- `supabase.auth.getUser()`
- load `app.profile`
- `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password })`
- `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'azure', options: { redirectTo } })`
- `supabase.auth.signOut()`
- subscribe to auth state changes

User object exposed to the app should preserve the fields UI expects:

```text
id
first_name or display_name parsing
last_name if available
email
avatar/avatar_url
role(s)
```

Prefer a new app profile type over pretending it is still `DirectusUser`.

### 8.3 Rewrite Login Page

Update `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx`:

- Replace `microsoftLoginUrl()` usage.
- Keep existing UI if possible.
- Microsoft button should call Supabase Azure OAuth.
- Email/password fallback should call Supabase password sign-in if still supported.
- Error copy can remain generic.

### 8.4 Rewrite CRM API Module

Rewrite `src/features/crm/api.ts` function by function. Keep exported function
names stable at first so pages/components do not all change at once.

Suggested migration order:

1. Auth/profile and app access.
2. `fetchRetailers` / `fetchIngestedDomains` / `updateRetailer`.
3. `fetchBuyers` / `fetchIngestedContacts` / `updateBuyer`.
4. `fetchDepartments`, `createDepartment`, `updateDepartment`, `deleteDepartment`.
5. `fetchOpportunities`, `setOpportunityStage`, `updateOpportunity`.
6. `fetchEmailMessages`, `updateEmailMessage`.
7. `fetchMeetingNotes`, `updateMeetingNote`.
8. `fetchIgnoreRules`, `createIgnoreRule`.
9. `fetchAiModelConfigs`, `updateAiModelConfig`.
10. `fetchNotes`, `createNote`, `updateNote`.
11. `fetchTasks`, `createTask`, `updateTask`.
12. `fetchApprovalThreads`, `updateApprovalThread`.
13. `askOpportunityAi` worker endpoint compatibility.

Use `api` views for reads where useful. Use base tables or RPCs for writes.

Example mapping:

```ts
// Directus
readItems('crm_opportunity', {
  fields: ['id', 'name', { retailer: ['id', 'name'] }],
})

// Supabase view/table approach
supabase
  .from('crm_opportunity_list') // if exposed under api and generated types allow it
  .select('*')

// Or schema-qualified direct table if needed
supabase
  .schema('crm')
  .from('opportunity')
  .select(`
    id,
    name,
    company:company_id(id, name)
  `)
```

Use the actual generated type and Supabase query syntax supported by the current
client version.

### 8.5 Preserve Loader Resilience

`CrmDataContext.load()` currently uses `Promise.allSettled`. This is intentional.

Do not regress to all-or-nothing loading.

Reason:

One bad collection/RLS issue should not blank every CRM page. A prior Directus
schema mismatch blanked the whole app when the loader used all-or-nothing logic.

Keep per-resource failure handling and useful diagnostics.

### 8.6 Types

Replace hand-maintained Directus-shaped types gradually.

Recommended pattern:

- Keep current UI/domain types temporarily.
- Add adapter functions from Supabase rows/views to UI types.
- Use generated `Database` types for Supabase query outputs.
- Avoid broad `any`.
- Do not rename all UI-facing types in the same commit as the backend switch unless necessary.

## Phase 9: CRM Worker Migration

The CRM worker currently logs into Directus and calls `/items/*`.

Port `/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-worker.mjs` to Supabase service-role access.

Keep the worker as a Node service initially. Do not move long-running ingestion
or summarization jobs to Edge Functions yet.

Worker responsibilities:

- Outlook ingest
- Email reroute
- Fireflies webhook server
- Contact sync
- Opportunity AI summaries
- Apply ignore rules
- Opportunity chat endpoint

Preserve public endpoints:

```text
https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app/health
/s/fireflies-webhook
/s/opportunity-chat
```

Server-only env:

```text
SUPABASE_URL=https://tcscehehgeiijilylezv.supabase.co  # preview first
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=<server-only key>
```

Never expose service-role key to browser code.

Directus helper replacement:

```js
// Current style
dx('GET', '/items/crm_email_message?...')
dx('POST', '/items/crm_email_message', body)
dx('PATCH', `/items/crm_email_message/${id}`, patch)

// Target style
supabase.from('email_message').select(...)
supabase.from('email_message').insert(...)
supabase.from('email_message').update(patch).eq('id', id)
```

If using non-public schemas with `supabase-js`, set schema correctly:

```js
supabase.schema('crm').from('email_message')
```

For complex filtering or multi-table writes, create service-role RPCs in
`shared-db` migrations.

Sensitive worker data:

- Do not expose raw email bodies broadly.
- Prefer `body_preview` / curated routing fields for browser views.
- Store raw payload references in `body_storage_ref`, `ingest.raw_record`, or service-only tables.

## Phase 10: Department/Company Integrity

Current Directus/Postgres SQL enforces that selected departments belong to the
selected account/company.

Equivalent constraints must exist in shared-db.

Rules:

- `crm.department.company_id` is required.
- Department names should be unique per company.
- `crm.opportunity.department_id`, if set, must belong to `crm.opportunity.company_id`.
- `crm.email_message.department_id`, if introduced, must belong to its company.
- `crm.meeting_note.department_id`, if introduced, must belong to its company.
- `crm.note.department_id`, if introduced, must belong to its company.
- `crm.task.department_id`, if introduced, must belong to its company.

If these fields are in metadata or omitted in baseline, add explicit columns and
triggers before the frontend allows editing them.

Reference current SQL:

```text
/worksp/directus/pm-system/sql/enforce-crm-department-scope.sql
```

## Phase 11: Realtime

Enable realtime only for user-facing state after RLS is correct.

Baseline realtime candidates include:

```text
app.comment
app.notification
app.activity
crm.opportunity
crm.task
crm.note
crm.email_message
dam.asset
dam.style_group
```

CRM should use realtime for:

- opportunity/stage movement
- task status/assignment changes
- notes/comments/activity
- email routing status changes

Do not stream:

- raw email bodies
- raw meeting transcripts
- `ingest.*`
- queue/admin tables
- PLM/RFQ/cost base tables

Realtime is optional for the first cutover if polling/bootstrap refresh is
sufficient. Do not block core migration on realtime unless the owner explicitly
requires it.

## Phase 12: Storage

Do not migrate object storage as part of the CRM backend cutover.

CRM account logos currently use logo.dev through `src/components/app/AccountLogo.tsx`.
There is no Directus logo file field to migrate.

as-is.

first database migration. Supabase Storage can be evaluated separately later.
