content.text:
The app is live and showing the new Supabase auth login page with "Continue with Microsoft". The migration is complete.

Here's what was accomplished this session:

- **shared-db PR #3 merged** — migrations locked into the repo's main branch
- **All 9 migrations applied to production** (`qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai`) including 4 baseline + 5 CRM-specific
- **PostgREST updated** to expose `api, crm, pim, core` schemas
- **Azure app registration created** ("POP CRM — Supabase Auth", tenant `1caeb1c0`) with Supabase callback URL, enabled on the project
- **GitHub secrets set** + Dockerfile/workflow updated to bake `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`/`VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` into the image
- **CI passed**, Coolify deployed, `crm.designflow.app` is live and serving the Supabase-backed CRM

The next things to do (when you're ready) are importing data from Directus and provisioning your user account in `app.profile` + `app.app_access` so you can actually log in and see data.

content.content:
begin
  foreach t in array array[
    'app.profile'::regclass,
    'app.user_role'::regclass,
    'app.app_access'::regclass,
    'app.file_object'::regclass,
    'app.comment'::regclass,
    'app.activity'::regclass,
--
end $$;

create policy profile_select_self_or_admin on app.profile
  for select to authenticated
  using (id = app.current_profile_id() or app.has_role('administrator'));

create policy profile_admin_write on app.profile
  for all to authenticated
  using (app.has_role('administrator'))
  with check (app.has_role('administrator'));

create policy notification_select_own on app.notification
  for select to authenticated
  using (profile_id = app.current_profile_id() or app.has_role('administrator'));

create policy notification_update_own_read_state on app.notification
  for update to authenticated
  using (profile_id = app.current_profile_id() or app.has_role('administrator'))
  with check (profile_id = app.current_profile_id() or app.has_role('administrator'));

do $$
declare
  t regclass;
begin

content.text:
I need a Directus admin token to pull the data. Can you provide a static admin token from `https://data.designflow.app`? (You can generate one in Directus → User → Token, or under Settings → Access Tokens.)

While you get that, let me handle the user provisioning — I'll write an auth trigger so that Microsoft SSO logins auto-create a profile, and pre-seed your administrator access:

input.name: crm_auth_provision

content.text: {"success":true}

Auth trigger is live — your first Microsoft SSO login will auto-create the profile and grant you administrator + CRM access.

Now for the Directus data import. Do you have a Directus static admin token? If not, the quickest way to get one: go to `https://data.designflow.app/admin/users`, open your user, scroll to Token, generate one, and paste it here. Or if you have the Directus admin email/password handy, I can authenticate via the API.

input.command:
DX="https://data.designflow.app"
TOKEN="eiXLlqvOw7QERUop9egycjXDytAGxxl6"
for col in retailer buyer crm_department crm_opportunity crm_meeting_note crm_note crm_task crm_email_message crm_ai_model_config crm_ignore_rule crm_licensor_approval_thread licensor property factory; do
  count=$(curl -s "$DX/items/$col?aggregate[count]=*&limit=1" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq -r '.data[0]["count(*)"] // "err"')
  echo "$col: $count"
done

3	//              crm_department, crm_meeting_note, crm_email_message
4

7

9	const DX_TOKEN = 'eiXLlqvOw7QERUop9egycjXDytAGxxl6'

11	const SB_KEY = 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6InFzbGx5ZXp0ZHdqZ2lyc3lzZ2FpIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImlhdCI6MTc4MTk4MzcxMywiZXhwIjoyMDk3NTU5NzEzfQ.pUg7tNPzEZmdiWM6bSS2N4wV259HCc7Gor3DkqV-MLo'
12	
13	const mkClient = (schema) => createClient(SB_URL, SB_KEY, { db: { schema }, realtime: { transport: ws } })
14	const sb = mkClient('public')
15	
16	async function dx(collection, params = '') {
17	  const rows = []
18	  let offset = 0
19	  const limit = 500
20	  while (true) {
21	    const r = await fetch(`${DX}/items/${collection}?limit=${limit}&offset=${offset}${params}`, {
22	      headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${DX_TOKEN}` }
23	    })
24	    const j = await r.json()
25	    if (!j.data?.length) break
26	    rows.push(...j.data)
27	    if (j.data.length < limit) break
28	    offset += limit
29	  }
30	  return rows
31	}
32	
33	async function upsert(table, rows, schema = 'public') {
34	  if (!rows.length) return
35	  const client = schema === 'public' ? sb : createClient(SB_URL, SB_KEY, { db: { schema } })
36	  const { error } = await client.from(table).upsert(rows, { onConflict: 'id' })
37	  if (error) throw new Error(`${schema}.${table}: ${error.message}`)
38	  console.log(`  ✓ ${schema}.${table}: ${rows.length} rows`)
39	}
40	
41	// ── 1. Retailers → core.company ──────────────────────────────────────────────
42	async function migrateRetailers() {
43	  console.log('\n[1/5] Retailers → core.company')
44	  const rows = await dx('retailer')
45	
46	  const companies = rows.map(r => ({
47	    // Use Directus UUID as the Supabase PK so department/buyer FKs can resolve
48	    id: r.id,
49	    name: r.name,
50	    domain: r.domain || null,
51	    company_type: 'customer',
52	    // CRM parity fields
53	    customer_status: r.customer_status || null,
54	    chain_type: r.chain_type || null,
55	    routing_aliases: r.routing_aliases || null,
56	    so_patterns: r.so_patterns || null,
57	    metadata: {
58	      directus_id: r.id,
59	      logo_url: r.logo_url || null,
60	      notes: r.notes || null,
61	      resale_restriction: r.resale_restriction || null,
62	      external_id: r.external_id || r.id,
63	      external_source: r.external_source || 'directus',
64	    },
65	  }))
66	
67	  const coreClient = mkClient('core')
68	  const { error } = await coreClient.from('company').upsert(companies, { onConflict: 'id' })
69	  if (error) throw new Error(`core.company: ${error.message}`)
70	  console.log(`  ✓ core.company: ${companies.length} rows`)
71	
72	  return new Map(rows.map(r => [r.id, r.id])) // dx_id → sb_id (same UUID)
73	}
74	
75	// ── 2. Departments → crm.department ──────────────────────────────────────────
76	async function migrateDepartments(companyMap) {
77	  console.log('\n[2/5] Departments → crm.department')
78	  const rows = await dx('crm_department')
79	
80	  const depts = rows.map(r => ({
81	    id: r.id,
82	    name: r.name,
83	    category: r.category || null,
84	    division: r.division || null,
85	    is_active: r.active !== false,
86	    sort_order: r.sort ?? null,
87	    company_id: r.retailer ? (companyMap.get(r.retailer) ?? null) : null,
88	    // primary_buyer resolved in pass after contacts are inserted
89	    metadata: {
90	      directus_id: r.id,
91	      external_id: r.external_id || r.id,
92	      external_source: r.external_source || 'directus',
93	    },
94	  }))
95	
96	  const crmClient = mkClient('crm')
97	  const { error } = await crmClient.from('department').upsert(depts, { onConflict: 'id' })
98	  if (error) throw new Error(`crm.department: ${error.message}`)
99	  console.log(`  ✓ crm.department: ${depts.length} rows`)
100	
101	  return new Map(rows.map(r => [r.id, r.id]))
102	}
103	
104	// ── 3. Buyers → core.contact + core.contact_company ─────────────────────────
105	async function migrateBuyers(companyMap, deptMap) {
106	  console.log('\n[3/5] Buyers → core.contact + core.contact_company')
107	  const rows = await dx('buyer')
108	
109	  const contacts = rows.map(r => ({
110	    id: r.id,
111	    full_name: r.name || [r.first_name, r.last_name].filter(Boolean).join(' ') || null,
112	    first_name: r.first_name || null,
113	    last_name: r.last_name || null,
114	    email: r.email || null,
115	    phone: r.phone || null,
116	    title: r.job_title || null,
117	    metadata: {
118	      directus_id: r.id,
119	      external_id: r.external_id || r.id,
120	      external_source: r.external_source || 'directus',
121	      samples_required: r.samples_required ?? null,
122	    },
123	  }))
124	
125	  const coreClient = mkClient('core')
126	  const { error: ce } = await coreClient.from('contact').upsert(contacts, { onConflict: 'id' })
127	  if (ce) throw new Error(`core.contact: ${ce.message}`)
128	  console.log(`  ✓ core.contact: ${contacts.length} rows`)
129	
130	  // contact_company junction rows
131	  const junctions = rows
132	    .filter(r => r.retailer)
133	    .map(r => ({
134	      contact_id: r.id,
135	      company_id: companyMap.get(r.retailer) ?? null,
136	      relationship_type: 'buyer',
137	      is_primary: true,
138	      contact_type: r.contact_type || null,
139	      scope: r.scope || null,
140	      crm_department_id: r.department ? (deptMap.get(r.department) ?? null) : null,
141	    }))
142	
143	  if (junctions.length) {
144	    const { error: je } = await coreClient.from('contact_company').upsert(junctions, {
145	      onConflict: 'contact_id,company_id,relationship_type',
146	    })
147	    if (je) throw new Error(`core.contact_company: ${je.message}`)
148	    console.log(`  ✓ core.contact_company: ${junctions.length} rows`)
149	  }
150	
151	  // Back-fill department.primary_contact_id
152	  const deptPrimaryMap = new Map()
153	  for (const r of rows) {
154	    if (r.department && !deptPrimaryMap.has(r.department)) {
155	      deptPrimaryMap.set(r.department, r.id)

157	  }
158	  const dxDepts = await dx('crm_department')

168	    console.log(`  ✓ crm.department primary_contact_id: ${deptsWithPrimary.length} updated`)
169	  }
170	
171	  return new Map(rows.map(r => [r.id, r.id]))
172	}

input.description: Check department table definition for company FK

content.content:
create table crm.department (
  id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  company_id uuid not null references core.company(id) on delete cascade,
  name text not null,
  primary_contact_id uuid references core.contact(id) on delete set null,
  status text not null default 'active',
  metadata jsonb not null default '{}'::jsonb,
  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (company_id, name)
);

create table crm.department (
  id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  company_id uuid not null references core.company(id) on delete cascade,
  name text not null,
  primary_contact_id uuid references core.contact(id) on delete set null,
  status text not null default 'active',

  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (company_id, name)
);

content.text: The FK is defined as `company_id uuid not null references core.company(id) on delete cascade` — so structurally departments cannot exist without a company. The PostgREST "no relationship" error is just a schema-cache limitation for cross-schema FKs (crm→core), not a missing constraint. select
  c.conname as constraint_name,
  pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) as definition,
  (select count(*) from crm.department) as total_departments,
  (select count(*) from crm.department d
     left join core.company co on co.id = d.company_id
     where co.id is null) as orphan_departments
from pg_constraint c
join pg_class t on t.oid = c.conrelid
join pg_namespace n on n.oid = t.relnamespace
where n.nspname = 'crm' and t.relname = 'department'
  and c.contype = 'f'
  and pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) like '%core.company%';

content.text:
Confirmed on all three counts:

**Departments are fully linked to and dependent on companies.**

- **FK constraint exists**: `department_company_id_fkey: FOREIGN KEY (company_id) REFERENCES core.company(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`
- **Column is `NOT NULL`**: a department literally cannot be inserted without a company
- **Data is clean**: all 38 departments resolve to a real company — **0 orphans**

The `ON DELETE CASCADE` also means if a company is deleted, its departments are automatically removed — exactly the "departments don't exist on their own" semantics you described. The earlier PostgREST "no relationship" message was only a schema-cache quirk for cross-schema embeds (crm→core), not a missing constraint.

One thing worth flagging: in Directus, 3 of the meeting notes and some other records referenced **deleted** retailers, which I nulled out during migration (since those companies no longer exist). Departments had no such issue — every one mapped to a live company.

Deployed successfully. Both fixes are live on `crm.designflow.app`:

1. **Sidebar footer** now reads "Supabase · qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co" instead of the stale Directus label. To be clear: the app was never actually connected to Directus after the rewrite — it was just a leftover hardcoded string. The real data layer is fully Supabase.

Give it a hard refresh (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+R) to pick up the new bundle. The "New Company" entries should drop out of Accounts and the Triage count should reflect them instead.

# AI Session Documentation Update Prompt

Use this at the end of an AI coding session when the session changed code, deployment behavior, data flow, configuration, operational assumptions, or important project knowledge.

Your job is not to rebuild the entire documentation system. 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Update only the Markdown files that need durable knowledge from this specific session.

## Goal

Record what this session learned or changed so future developers and future AI sessions do not have to rediscover it.

Focus on:
- Information from this session that will still matter later.
- Idiosyncrasies that may look wrong but are intentional.
- Mistakes, dead ends, or assumptions from this session that future sessions should avoid.
- Architecture, data flow, deployment, configuration, or workflow changes.
- New operational steps, rollback notes, feature flags, identifiers, limits, or dependencies.
- Any temporary/incomplete work that needs a handoff.

## Rules

- Derive every doc update from code, config, migrations, workflows, commands run, or verified session findings.
- Do not document guesses as facts. Mark unknowns clearly and say how to verify them.
- Do not add secrets, tokens, passwords, private keys, or credential values.
- Do not duplicate large explanations across multiple docs. Put the short routing/context note in `AGENTS.md` only if future AI sessions need to see it early; put topic detail in the relevant doc under `docs/`.
- If the change does not require documentation updates, say so explicitly in the final report.

## What To Update

- `AGENTS.md`: only for high-signal guidance future AI sessions must see quickly, including new quirks, critical warnings, task routing, identifiers, or "do not repeat this mistake" notes.
- `HANDOFF.md`: create or update only if work is unfinished, blocked, partially deployed, or requires continuation context. Delete it only when the unfinished work it describes is truly complete.
- Topic docs under `docs/`: update the specific doc for the affected area, such as architecture, deployment, configuration, schema, worker logic, PopSG, Helper, Seafile, auth, bulk jobs, or known quirks.
- `README.md`: update only if the quick-start or top-level orientation changed.
- `CLAUDE.md`: update only for Claude Code-specific workflow rules. General AI/developer guidance belongs in `AGENTS.md`.

## Shared supabase.com Database / Backend Changes

If this session made any database, Supabase, migration, API contract, backend data-flow, auth/RLS, realtime, worker, or shared-backend change having to do with the shared supabase.com backend, also update the canonical shared database repo:

```text

u2giants/shared-db

https://github.com/u2giants/shared-db

```

This applies even when the code change was made inside an app repo. App repos may contain generated types, frontend adapters, workers, or local notes, but durable backend truth belongs in `shared-db`.

At minimum, document in `u2giants/shared-db`:

- What changed, including tables, columns, views, RPCs, triggers, functions, RLS policies, realtime publications, storage assumptions, worker/backend behavior, generated database types, or app-facing API contracts.

- Why it changed, including the product need, collision risk, bug, migration constraint, compatibility reason, or cross-app dependency.

- Which app or apps are affected: CRM, DAM, PM/PIM, Directus/PLM, workers, scripts, or shared admin tooling.

- Where the durable implementation lives: migration filenames, SQL files, scripts, API views/RPC names, worker files, generated type files, and relevant app repo commits if known.

- What was verified: local checks, `scripts/check-sql.sh`, Supabase dry-runs, preview branch pushes, app builds, smoke tests, RLS/role tests, data-parity checks, or reasons verification could not be completed.

- What remains risky or unfinished: production promotion status, data backfill needs, RLS gaps, app screens not migrated, deprecated contracts, rollback notes, or follow-up migrations.

Use the existing `shared-db` documentation structure:

- Put cross-app operating rules or urgent warnings in `AGENTS.md` only when future AI sessions must see them immediately.

- Put schema/API ownership, relationships, migration risks, or implementation notes in the matching doc under `docs/`.

- Put app migration handoffs under `docs/app-migration-notes/<app>-YYYYMMDD.md`.

- Put verification evidence under `docs/verification/` when it records a reusable database or preview-branch check.

- Add or update timestamped migrations under `supabase/migrations/`; never edit a migration that may already have been applied.

If a backend-relevant change was intentionally not documented in `shared-db`, state that explicitly in the final report and explain why.

## Required Session Note Shape

When adding session-derived knowledge, prefer concise entries like:

```md
### [Short decision or warning name]

What changed:
[One or two sentences.]

Why:
[Constraint, incident, bug, user decision, or operational reason.]

Future sessions should:
[Concrete instruction: what to do, what to avoid, where to verify.]
```

For unfinished work, include:

```md
## [Workstream name]

Status:
[done / partial / blocked / not started]

Done:
[Verified completed items.]

Next action:
[Exact next step.]

Risks / watchouts:
[Anything future sessions must not miss.]
```

## Verification Before Final Response

- Docs changed are limited to files that actually needed updates.
- New claims match the repository or verified session findings.
- No secrets were added.
- `HANDOFF.md` exists only if continuation context is needed.
- Any new quirk or warning explains why it exists and what would break if ignored.

## Final Report

Reply with:

```md
## Documentation Updates
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `path/to/file.md` | Short summary |

## Handoff
- `HANDOFF.md`: present/absent
- Reason: ...

## Verification
- Docs verified against session findings: yes/no
- Secrets added: yes/no
- Unknowns remaining: ...
```



Before you finish, make sure no repo has untracked files, especially /worksp/shared-db. If you create a shared-db migration, put it on its own branch or clean it up.
Before you end, leave the repo in a handoff-safe state.

If work is complete:
- run the required checks
- commit/merge/push/deploy according to the repo instructions
- confirm the working tree is clean, or list exactly which untracked/modified files remain and why

If work is not complete:
- do not leave mystery untracked files
- create or update HANDOFF.md
- list every changed/untracked file, what it is for, whether it is safe to keep, and the next exact action
- do not say “done” if anything still needs commit/merge/apply
Because shared-db affects multiple apps, do not leave untracked migrations or docs without either finishing the branch/PR/merge workflow or writing HANDOFF.md explaining exactly what remains.

file.content:
# HANDOFF — popcrm-web

Continuation context for unfinished/partially-deployed work. Delete this file
once the item below is truly complete. See `AGENTS.md` for the full operating guide.

## Contact relationship clear RPC

Status:
partial — frontend is backward-compatible and safe to deploy, but the new clear
behavior depends on a shared-db migration that still needs to be applied through
the shared database workflow.

Done:
- Added app-repo copy of
  `shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260623024500_crm_update_contact_clear_relationship_fields.sql`.
- Added canonical `/worksp/shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260623024500_crm_update_contact_clear_relationship_fields.sql`.
- Updated `src/features/crm/api.ts` so `updateBuyer` sends explicit `p_clear_*`
  flags and retries the old RPC argument shape if production has not applied the
  new function signature yet.
- Updated Contacts/Data Admin so relationship-owned contact edits send account
  context, and department selection can infer the account when the row was
  previously unassigned.
- `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, and `/worksp/shared-db/scripts/check-sql.sh`
  static checks passed.

Next action:
1. In canonical `u2giants/shared-db`, review and land migration
   `20260623024500_crm_update_contact_clear_relationship_fields.sql` using the
   shared-db branch/PR workflow.
2. Apply/promote it to production only after the shared-db preview/verification
   checklist passes.
3. After production has the new `api.crm_update_contact` signature, verify:
   clearing Contact Type in Data Admin persists after refetch, clearing a
   Department persists, and changing a contact's Account updates the segmented
   Contacts counts correctly.
4. Once verified, this `HANDOFF.md` can be deleted.

Risks / watchouts:
- Do not remove the old-RPC fallback in `updateBuyer` until production definitely
  has the new function signature.
- `core.contact_company.company_id` is required; relationship-owned contact
  fields must be updated against a specific account relationship row.
- Passing `null` alone is not a reliable clear operation for this RPC contract;
  use the explicit `p_clear_*` flags.

content.content:
1	# popcrm-web Supabase Migration — Session Handoff (2026-06-21)
2	
3	CRM (`u2giants/popcrm-web`) migrated from the Directus backend to the shared
4	Supabase project, **preview-complete**: schema + frontend + worker are done and
5	validated locally; nothing touches production. The live `db push` to the preview
6	branch is the one remaining step (see Known Gaps).
7	
8	## New migrations (this PR)
9	
10	| File | Purpose |
11	|---|---|
12	| `20260621151239_crm_parity_fields.sql` | Adds the explicit columns the CRM UI/worker use onto `core.company`, `core.contact_company`, and `crm.*` (customer_status, chain_type, routing_aliases/so_patterns, opportunity program/season/division/incoterms/AI fields, email routing fields, meeting/note/task/approval fields, etc.). Additive + nullable. |
13	| `20260621151254_crm_integrity_triggers.sql` | Department-belongs-to-company enforcement (parity with Directus `enforce-crm-department-scope.sql`) on opportunity/email/meeting/note/task and `core.contact_company`. |
14	| `20260621151327_crm_api_views.sql` | `security_invoker` browser views, one per screen (see table below). No raw email bodies / transcripts / ingest payloads. |
15	| `20260621151359_crm_api_rpcs.sql` | `current_user_profile()` identity contract; guarded `crm_update_account` / `crm_update_contact` (core writes) and `crm_set_opportunity_stage`. |
16	| `20260621151419_crm_rls_realtime.sql` | `profile_select_staff` policy (assignee/owner display); realtime for meeting_note/department/approval; **exposes `api, crm, pim, core` schemas to PostgREST**. |
17	| `20260622043000_crm_contact_segments.sql` | Preserves `api.crm_contact_list`, adds explicit CRM access gating, and adds `api.crm_contact_segment_list` / `api.crm_contact_segment_counts` so the Contacts page can load Customer, Department, and Triage slices without eager-loading All contacts. |
18	
19	Validated by applying the full chain (4 baseline + these 5) to a throwaway
20	Postgres 15 with Supabase auth stubs: all apply cleanly; integrity trigger,
21	views, and RPC guards verified functionally.
22	
23	## Tables / views / RPCs per CRM screen
24	
25	| Screen | Reads | Writes |
26	|---|---|---|
27	| Overview | `api.crm_account_overview`, derived from the lists below | — |
28	| Accounts (triage) | `api.crm_account_list` (all companies) | `api.crm_update_account` RPC |
29	| Accounts pickers (customers) | `api.crm_account_list` filtered `customer_status in (ACTIVE_CUSTOMER, POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER)` | — |
30	| Contacts | `api.crm_contact_segment_list`, `api.crm_contact_segment_counts`; `api.crm_contact_list` remains the generic contact contract | `api.crm_update_contact` RPC |
31	| Departments | `api.crm_department_list` | `crm.department` (direct) |
32	| Pipeline / Programs | `api.crm_opportunity_list` | `crm.opportunity` (direct), `api.crm_set_opportunity_stage` |
33	| Email Routing | `api.crm_email_routing_queue` | `crm.email_message` (direct), `crm.ignore_rule` |
34	| Meetings | `api.crm_meeting_list` | `crm.meeting_note` |
35	| Notes | `api.crm_note_list` | `crm.note` |
36	| Tasks | `api.crm_task_list` | `crm.task` |
37	| Approvals | `api.crm_approval_queue` | `crm.licensor_approval_thread` |
38	| Settings | `api.crm_ai_model_config_list`, `api.crm_ignore_rule_list` | `crm.ai_model_config` |
39	| Auth | `api.current_user_profile()` RPC | Supabase Auth (Azure OAuth + password) |
40	
41	The "curated customers vs full ingested registry" split (Directus
42	`retailer`/`buyer` vs `ingested_domains`/`ingested_contact`) is collapsed to one
43	`core.company`/`core.contact`, filtered by `customer_status` in the frontend.
44	
45	## Contacts segmented API contract
46	
47	The CRM Contacts page should not load all contacts merely to classify rows into
48	tabs. The database owns that classification through
49	`api.crm_contact_segment_list.crm_segment`:
50	
51	| Segment | Definition |
52	|---|---|
53	| `customer` | The contact's primary account has `customer_status in (ACTIVE_CUSTOMER, POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER)` and the primary relationship has no `crm_department_id`. |
54	| `department` | The contact's primary account has `customer_status in (ACTIVE_CUSTOMER, POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER)` and the primary relationship has a `crm_department_id`. |
55	| `triage` | Every other contact: no customer account, an untriaged account, or a reviewed non-customer account. |
56	| `all` | Count-only row in `api.crm_contact_segment_counts`; clients can load all rows from `api.crm_contact_segment_list` only after the user opens the All tab. |
57	
58	`api.crm_contact_segment_list` keeps the same business columns as
59	`api.crm_contact_list` and appends only `crm_segment`, so frontend adapters can
60	continue using the same contact mapper. Counts come from
61	`api.crm_contact_segment_counts` so tab badges do not require a full contact load.
62	
63	Access is intentionally explicit: both views are `security_invoker = false` and
64	contain the `app.has_app_access('crm')` gate through `api.crm_contact_list`. This
65	matches the browser-safe production contract and prevents profile/app-access gaps
66	from being mistaken for missing data. Realtime consumers must refetch the relevant
67	view after a short debounce; base-table realtime payloads do not include joined
68	company/department display fields.
69	
70	Do not add server-side ordering by derived fields such as contact `name` to these
71	views or frontend queries. The previous Supabase cutover incident timed out on a
72	paged browser load when derived-field ordering/filtering was pushed through
73	PostgREST. Fetch the segment slice and sort/filter within the browser table.
74	
75	## RLS changes
76	
77	- `app.profile`: added `profile_select_staff` (any role can read profiles) so the
78	  CRM can display assignee/owner/salesperson names under `security_invoker` views.
79	- Account/contact writes go through `security definer` RPCs guarded by
80	  `app.has_app_access('crm')`, so baseline `core` admin-only write RLS stays intact.
81	- `crm.*` writes rely on the existing baseline `crm_write` policy (sales/licensing/admin).
82	
83	## Realtime
84	
85	Baseline already publishes `crm.opportunity/task/note/email_message`. Added
86	`crm.meeting_note`, `crm.department`, `crm.licensor_approval_thread`.
87	
88	## Frontend (popcrm-web, committed in that repo)
89	
90	- Removed `@directus/sdk`; added `@supabase/supabase-js`.
91	- New `src/lib/supabase.ts`; generated `src/lib/database.types.ts` (from the
92	  validated local schema — regenerate from preview once reachable).
93	- Rewrote `src/auth/auth.tsx` (Supabase Auth + `current_user_profile`),
94	  `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` (Azure OAuth), `src/features/crm/api.ts` (api views +
95	  adapters + mapped writes), `src/lib/types.ts` (`AppUser`).
96	- `CrmDataContext` `Promise.allSettled` resilience preserved.
97	- `npm run build` passes; `npm run lint` 0 errors, 0 new warnings.
98	- Env: `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`, `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN`.
99	
100	## Worker
101	
102	`/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-worker-supabase.mjs` — full Supabase service-role
103	port (outlook-ingest, reroute, fireflies-server, contact-sync, summarize,
104	apply-ignore-rules); endpoints `/health`, `/s/fireflies-webhook`,
105	`/s/opportunity-chat` preserved (chat now verifies a Supabase JWT Bearer token).
106	The original Directus `crm-worker.mjs` is kept for rollback. Worker runtime needs
107	`npm i @supabase/supabase-js` and `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`.
108	
109	## Data migration / reconciliation
110	
111	Not performed (no production Directus dump in scope this session). The preview
112	branch has no CRM data yet, so smoke tests need either a data load or seed rows.
113	
114	## Preview test results
115	
116	Not run: the preview branch DB (`xjcyeuvzkhtzsheknaiu`) is IPv6-only and was
117	unreachable from the migration environment (`ECONNREFUSED`) via both the Supabase
118	CLI and the management API, and no preview DB password was available. SQL was
119	instead validated on a local Postgres 15 (see above).
120	
121	## Production migration status
122	
123	These migrations are present in the production Supabase migration ledger:
124	
125	```text
126	20260621150714_foundation.sql              (baseline)
127	20260621150815_app_core.sql                (baseline)
128	20260621151024_domain_tables.sql           (baseline)
129	20260621151155_api_rls_realtime.sql        (baseline)
130	20260621151239_crm_parity_fields.sql
131	20260621151254_crm_integrity_triggers.sql
132	20260621151327_crm_api_views.sql
133	20260621151359_crm_api_rpcs.sql
134	20260621151419_crm_rls_realtime.sql
135	20260622043000_crm_contact_segments.sql    (Contacts segmented API)
136	```
137	
138	The production project also has older PopDAM migration versions. This repo keeps
139	no-op marker files for those legacy versions so Supabase CLI can run future
140	`supabase db push --dry-run` checks normally.
141	
142	## Known gaps
143	
144	- **Regenerate `database.types.ts`** from production or the active preview branch after schema changes and update app repos that rely on generated types.
145	- **Phase 5 identity**: configure the Azure provider in Supabase Auth and seed `app.profile` / `app.user_role` / `app.app_access` for CRM users — without a provisioned profile a signed-in user has no CRM access and lists come back empty.
146	- **Data import + reconciliation** (Phase 7) and **role-based RLS tests** (Phase 6) still to run against preview.
147	- `crm.note.opportunity_id` is `on delete cascade` (baseline) and `crm.note` has no `factory`; meeting attendees have no shared table (stored in `meeting_note.metadata`).
148	- RPC `coalesce` semantics mean passing `null` does not clear a contact field (edge case).
149	- No CRM screen or worker command still depends on Directus; the Directus worker/backend remain only as read-only rollback.
150	
151	## Verification checklist for contact segments
152	
153	Run these on preview before production promotion:
154	
155	1. Confirm the views exist and PostgREST has reloaded:
156	
157	```sql
158	select table_schema, table_name
159	from information_schema.views
160	where table_schema = 'api'
161	  and table_name in (
162	    'crm_contact_list',
163	    'crm_contact_segment_list',
164	    'crm_contact_segment_counts'
165	  )
166	order by table_name;
167	```
168	
169	2. Confirm segment counts reconcile:
170	
171	```sql
172	select sum(contact_count) filter (where crm_segment <> 'all') as segmented_total,
173	       max(contact_count) filter (where crm_segment = 'all') as all_total
174	from api.crm_contact_segment_counts;
175	```
176	
177	3. Confirm the segmented view preserves the base contact count:
178	
179	```sql
180	select
181	  (select count(*) from api.crm_contact_list) as base_count,
182	  (select count(*) from api.crm_contact_segment_list) as segmented_count;
183	```
184	
185	4. Test with an authenticated CRM user, not service role, because
186	   `app.profile.auth_user_id` and `app.app_access` are part of the contract.
187

file.content:
# popcrm-web Supabase Migration — Session Handoff (2026-06-21)

CRM (`u2giants/popcrm-web`) migrated from the Directus backend to the shared
Supabase project, **preview-complete**: schema + frontend + worker are done and
validated locally; nothing touches production. The live `db push` to the preview
branch is the one remaining step (see Known Gaps).

## New migrations (this PR)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `20260621151239_crm_parity_fields.sql` | Adds the explicit columns the CRM UI/worker use onto `core.company`, `core.contact_company`, and `crm.*` (customer_status, chain_type, routing_aliases/so_patterns, opportunity program/season/division/incoterms/AI fields, email routing fields, meeting/note/task/approval fields, etc.). Additive + nullable. |
| `20260621151254_crm_integrity_triggers.sql` | Department-belongs-to-company enforcement (parity with Directus `enforce-crm-department-scope.sql`) on opportunity/email/meeting/note/task and `core.contact_company`. |
| `20260621151327_crm_api_views.sql` | `security_invoker` browser views, one per screen (see table below). No raw email bodies / transcripts / ingest payloads. |
| `20260621151359_crm_api_rpcs.sql` | `current_user_profile()` identity contract; guarded `crm_update_account` / `crm_update_contact` (core writes) and `crm_set_opportunity_stage`. |
| `20260621151419_crm_rls_realtime.sql` | `profile_select_staff` policy (assignee/owner display); realtime for meeting_note/department/approval; **exposes `api, crm, pim, core` schemas to PostgREST**. |
| `20260622043000_crm_contact_segments.sql` | Preserves `api.crm_contact_list`, adds explicit CRM access gating, and adds `api.crm_contact_segment_list` / `api.crm_contact_segment_counts` so the Contacts page can load Customer, Department, and Triage slices without eager-loading All contacts. |

Validated by applying the full chain (4 baseline + these 5) to a throwaway
Postgres 15 with Supabase auth stubs: all apply cleanly; integrity trigger,
views, and RPC guards verified functionally.

## Tables / views / RPCs per CRM screen

| Screen | Reads | Writes |
|---|---|---|
| Overview | `api.crm_account_overview`, derived from the lists below | — |
| Accounts (triage) | `api.crm_account_list` (all companies) | `api.crm_update_account` RPC |
| Accounts pickers (customers) | `api.crm_account_list` filtered `customer_status in (ACTIVE_CUSTOMER, POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER)` | — |
| Contacts | `api.crm_contact_segment_list`, `api.crm_contact_segment_counts`; `api.crm_contact_list` remains the generic contact contract | `api.crm_update_contact` RPC |
| Departments | `api.crm_department_list` | `crm.department` (direct) |
| Pipeline / Programs | `api.crm_opportunity_list` | `crm.opportunity` (direct), `api.crm_set_opportunity_stage` |
| Email Routing | `api.crm_email_routing_queue` | `crm.email_message` (direct), `crm.ignore_rule` |
| Meetings | `api.crm_meeting_list` | `crm.meeting_note` |
| Notes | `api.crm_note_list` | `crm.note` |
| Tasks | `api.crm_task_list` | `crm.task` |
| Approvals | `api.crm_approval_queue` | `crm.licensor_approval_thread` |
| Settings | `api.crm_ai_model_config_list`, `api.crm_ignore_rule_list` | `crm.ai_model_config` |
| Auth | `api.current_user_profile()` RPC | Supabase Auth (Azure OAuth + password) |

The "curated customers vs full ingested registry" split (Directus
`retailer`/`buyer` vs `ingested_domains`/`ingested_contact`) is collapsed to one
`core.company`/`core.contact`, filtered by `customer_status` in the frontend.

## Contacts segmented API contract

The CRM Contacts page should not load all contacts merely to classify rows into
tabs. The database owns that classification through
`api.crm_contact_segment_list.crm_segment`:

| Segment | Definition |
|---|---|
| `customer` | The contact's primary account has `customer_status in (ACTIVE_CUSTOMER, POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER)` and the primary relationship has no `crm_department_id`. |
| `department` | The contact's primary account has `customer_status in (ACTIVE_CUSTOMER, POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER)` and the primary relationship has a `crm_department_id`. |
| `triage` | Every other contact: no customer account, an untriaged account, or a reviewed non-customer account. |
| `all` | Count-only row in `api.crm_contact_segment_counts`; clients can load all rows from `api.crm_contact_segment_list` only after the user opens the All tab. |

`api.crm_contact_segment_list` keeps the same business columns as
`api.crm_contact_list` and appends only `crm_segment`, so frontend adapters can
continue using the same contact mapper. Counts come from
`api.crm_contact_segment_counts` so tab badges do not require a full contact load.

Access is intentionally explicit: both views are `security_invoker = false` and
contain the `app.has_app_access('crm')` gate through `api.crm_contact_list`. This
matches the browser-safe production contract and prevents profile/app-access gaps
from being mistaken for missing data. Realtime consumers must refetch the relevant
view after a short debounce; base-table realtime payloads do not include joined
company/department display fields.

Do not add server-side ordering by derived fields such as contact `name` to these
views or frontend queries. The previous Supabase cutover incident timed out on a
paged browser load when derived-field ordering/filtering was pushed through
PostgREST. Fetch the segment slice and sort/filter within the browser table.

## RLS changes

- `app.profile`: added `profile_select_staff` (any role can read profiles) so the
  CRM can display assignee/owner/salesperson names under `security_invoker` views.
- Account/contact writes go through `security definer` RPCs guarded by
  `app.has_app_access('crm')`, so baseline `core` admin-only write RLS stays intact.
- `crm.*` writes rely on the existing baseline `crm_write` policy (sales/licensing/admin).

## Realtime

Baseline already publishes `crm.opportunity/task/note/email_message`. Added
`crm.meeting_note`, `crm.department`, `crm.licensor_approval_thread`.

## Frontend (popcrm-web, committed in that repo)

- Removed `@directus/sdk`; added `@supabase/supabase-js`.
- New `src/lib/supabase.ts`; generated `src/lib/database.types.ts` (from the
  validated local schema — regenerate from preview once reachable).
- Rewrote `src/auth/auth.tsx` (Supabase Auth + `current_user_profile`),
  `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` (Azure OAuth), `src/features/crm/api.ts` (api views +
  adapters + mapped writes), `src/lib/types.ts` (`AppUser`).
- `CrmDataContext` `Promise.allSettled` resilience preserved.
- `npm run build` passes; `npm run lint` 0 errors, 0 new warnings.
- Env: `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`, `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN`.

## Worker

`/worksp/directus/pm-system/crm-worker-supabase.mjs` — full Supabase service-role
port (outlook-ingest, reroute, fireflies-server, contact-sync, summarize,
apply-ignore-rules); endpoints `/health`, `/s/fireflies-webhook`,
`/s/opportunity-chat` preserved (chat now verifies a Supabase JWT Bearer token).
The original Directus `crm-worker.mjs` is kept for rollback. Worker runtime needs
`npm i @supabase/supabase-js` and `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`.

## Data migration / reconciliation

Not performed (no production Directus dump in scope this session). The preview
branch has no CRM data yet, so smoke tests need either a data load or seed rows.

## Preview test results

Not run: the preview branch DB (`xjcyeuvzkhtzsheknaiu`) is IPv6-only and was
unreachable from the migration environment (`ECONNREFUSED`) via both the Supabase
CLI and the management API, and no preview DB password was available. SQL was
instead validated on a local Postgres 15 (see above).

## Production migration status

These migrations are present in the production Supabase migration ledger:

```text
20260621150714_foundation.sql              (baseline)
20260621150815_app_core.sql                (baseline)
20260621151024_domain_tables.sql           (baseline)
20260621151155_api_rls_realtime.sql        (baseline)
20260621151239_crm_parity_fields.sql
20260621151254_crm_integrity_triggers.sql
20260621151327_crm_api_views.sql
20260621151359_crm_api_rpcs.sql
20260621151419_crm_rls_realtime.sql
20260622043000_crm_contact_segments.sql    (Contacts segmented API)
```

The production project also has older PopDAM migration versions. This repo keeps
no-op marker files for those legacy versions so Supabase CLI can run future
`supabase db push --dry-run` checks normally.

## Known gaps

- **Regenerate `database.types.ts`** from production or the active preview branch after schema changes and update app repos that rely on generated types.
- **Phase 5 identity**: configure the Azure provider in Supabase Auth and seed `app.profile` / `app.user_role` / `app.app_access` for CRM users — without a provisioned profile a signed-in user has no CRM access and lists come back empty.
- **Data import + reconciliation** (Phase 7) and **role-based RLS tests** (Phase 6) still to run against preview.
- `crm.note.opportunity_id` is `on delete cascade` (baseline) and `crm.note` has no `factory`; meeting attendees have no shared table (stored in `meeting_note.metadata`).
- RPC `coalesce` semantics mean passing `null` does not clear a contact field (edge case).
- No CRM screen or worker command still depends on Directus; the Directus worker/backend remain only as read-only rollback.

## Verification checklist for contact segments

Run these on preview before production promotion:

1. Confirm the views exist and PostgREST has reloaded:

```sql
select table_schema, table_name
from information_schema.views
where table_schema = 'api'
  and table_name in (
    'crm_contact_list',
    'crm_contact_segment_list',
    'crm_contact_segment_counts'
  )
order by table_name;
```

2. Confirm segment counts reconcile:

```sql
select sum(contact_count) filter (where crm_segment <> 'all') as segmented_total,
       max(contact_count) filter (where crm_segment = 'all') as all_total
from api.crm_contact_segment_counts;
```

3. Confirm the segmented view preserves the base contact count:

```sql
select
  (select count(*) from api.crm_contact_list) as base_count,
  (select count(*) from api.crm_contact_segment_list) as segmented_count;
```

4. Test with an authenticated CRM user, not service role, because
   `app.profile.auth_user_id` and `app.app_access` are part of the contract.

# Configuration — popcrm-web

Infrastructure/server ownership notes are mirrored in
[`u2giants/albert-standards/infrastructure`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/tree/main/infrastructure).
If a config change affects deployment, runtime ownership, domains, or server
operations, update that shared reference too.

## Frontend Environment

Optional:

```bash
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<public anon key>
# logo.dev publishable token for domain-derived account logos (client-safe).
# When unset, accounts show name-initials avatars instead.
VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN=pk_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
```

`VITE_SUPABASE_URL` and `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` are required by
`src/lib/supabase.ts`; the app throws during startup if either is missing. The
anon key is public client configuration, not a service-role key.

`VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` is read by `src/components/app/AccountLogo.tsx`. It is a
**publishable** logo.dev key (safe to ship in client JS). Account logos are
fetched from `img.logo.dev` keyed on each retailer's `domain`, with a graceful
fallback to the initials avatar when there is no domain, no token, or the image
fails to load. In production it is supplied via the GitHub Actions secret
`LOGODEV_TOKEN`, passed as a Docker build-arg in `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`.
The 1Password source of truth is
`op://vibe_coding/logo.dev publishable token - popcrm-web/password`; the item
notes include rotation, verification, and future-AI guidance. Do not put the
token value itself in repo docs.

Because this is a static Vite app, `VITE_*` values are baked into the build. Rebuild and redeploy after changing them.

## Backend Requirements

Supabase Auth must allow the CRM frontend origins:

- `https://crm.designflow.app`
- `https://crm-dev.designflow.app`

Supabase must expose the `api`, `crm`, `pim`, and `core` schemas through
PostgREST for the CRM views/RPCs used by the app.

Supabase Auth must have the Azure provider configured for staff sign-in. A
signed-in browser session is not enough by itself: the user's `auth.users.id`
must be linked from `app.profile.auth_user_id`, and the profile must have
`app.app_access` for `crm`. If one user sees empty CRM lists while service-role
or admin checks show rows, verify that profile/app-access mapping first.

Backend secrets, service-role keys, and OAuth configuration live outside this
frontend repo. The frontend must never receive or bake a Supabase service-role
key.

Do not put secrets in this repo.

1	# Deployment — popcrm-web
2	
3	POP CRM is deployed through the compliant CI/CD path: **GitHub Actions builds
4	and publishes the image; Coolify pulls and runs it.** GitHub Actions never SSHes
5	into or mutates the production server.
6	
7	Shared server/infrastructure standards for the designflow.app VPS live in
8	[`u2giants/albert-standards/infrastructure`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/tree/main/infrastructure).
9	When deployment topology, domains, Coolify ownership, server dependencies,
10	break-glass actions, or infrastructure incident runbooks change here, update that
11	repository as part of the same documentation pass.
12	
13	## Normal release path
14	
15	```text
16	push to main
17	  ↓
18	GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/deploy.yml)
19	  verify        – npm ci, npm run lint, npm run build (tsc -b && vite build)
20	  build-and-push – Docker build (build-args: COMMIT_HASH/DATE,
21	                   VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY,
22	                   VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN),
23	                   push to GHCR (tags: latest, main, sha-<sha>)
24	  deploy         – POST Coolify deploy API, then wait for crm.designflow.app
25	  ↓
26	Coolify (https://coolify.designflow.app)
27	  pulls ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web:latest and updates the container
28	  ↓
29	crm.designflow.app  (runtime host only)
30	```
31	
32	Every release is traceable from: GitHub Actions run history → the `sha-<commit>`
33	tag in GHCR → Coolify's deployment history → the repo commit. The running build's
34	commit + timestamp are also shown in the app header (top bar).
35	
36	To release: merge/commit to `main`. That's it. Documentation / AI-context-only
37	changes (`docs/**`, `**/*.md`, `.claudeignore`, `.cursorignore`, `.copilotignore`)
38	are path-ignored and do not trigger a build.
39	
40	## Topology (the facts an owner needs to audit / recreate)
41	
42	| Item | Value |
43	|---|---|
44	| Deployment platform | Coolify `https://coolify.designflow.app` |
45	| Project | POP Creations CRM (`yp84tp0tmmshhcebgsd4j463`) |
46	| Environment | production |
47	| Coolify application | `popcrm-web` — uuid `a1vb55by4benmh25nd4ga8pt` |
48	| Server (runtime host) | localhost (`onwp0kd7w1w74w9yeotnoihp`) |
49	| Registry image | `ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web` (public package) |
50	| Image tag pattern | `latest`, `main`, `sha-<commit-sha>` |
51	| Production domains | `crm.designflow.app`, `crm-dev.designflow.app` |
52	| Deploy trigger | GitHub Actions → `POST {COOLIFY_BASE_URL}/api/v1/deploy?uuid=<app>` |
53	| Runtime config owner | Coolify (domains, ports, restart policy, health) |
54	| Build-time config | `VITE_*` (baked into the static bundle — see note) |
55	| Rollback | redeploy a previous `sha-<sha>` image via Coolify |
56	
57	### GitHub Actions secrets (CI/CD only)
58	
59	- `COOLIFY_BASE_URL` — `https://coolify.designflow.app`
60	- `COOLIFY_API_TOKEN` — Coolify API token used to trigger the deploy
61	- `COOLIFY_SERVER_UUID` — the Coolify **application** uuid (`a1vb55by4benmh25nd4ga8pt`)
62	- `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` — Supabase project URL baked into the static bundle
63	- `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` — Supabase public anon key baked into the static
64	  bundle. This is client configuration, not a service-role key.
65	- `LOGODEV_TOKEN` — logo.dev **publishable** token, passed as the
66	  `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` Docker build-arg (`deploy.yml` → Dockerfile) so account
67	  logos bake into the bundle. **Optional**: if unset the build still succeeds and
68	  accounts render initials avatars. Client-safe (publishable), not a real secret.
69	
70	The image is pushed to GHCR with the workflow's built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN`
71	(`packages: write`); no registry PAT is stored. Because `popcrm-web` is a public
72	repo, its GHCR package is public, so Coolify pulls it anonymously.
73	
74	### Runtime config note (static SPA)
75	
76	This is a static site, so its few `VITE_*` values are **build-time**, baked into
77	the bundle at `npm run build`. `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` and
78	`VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` are required by `src/lib/supabase.ts`; changing them
79	requires rebuilding and redeploying. This is the one documented exception to
80	"runtime config lives in Coolify" (CI/CD rules §19) — a static bundle has no
81	server-side runtime env. No service-role keys or backend secrets are baked in.
82	
83	## Rollback
84	
85	Preferred (CI/CD rules §6.3): in Coolify, redeploy the `popcrm-web` application
86	pinned to a previous immutable tag, e.g. `ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web:sha-<old>`.
87	Do **not** roll back by hand-editing containers or running `docker run` on the host.
88	
89	## Verify a release
90	
91	```bash
92	curl -fsS https://crm.designflow.app | rg -o '<title>[^<]+'      # <title>POP CRM
93	curl -fsS https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app/health            # Fireflies webhook
94	# The header in the app shows the deployed commit + NYC time.
95	```
96	
97	## Troubleshoot 502 after deploy
98	
99	On 2026-06-22, a deploy succeeded and the app container was healthy, but
100	`crm.designflow.app` returned 502 because `coolify-proxy`/Traefik lost access to
101	the Docker socket and could not discover the new upstream container.
102	
103	Use this order before rebuilding or rolling back:
104	
105	```bash
106	docker ps --format '{{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Status}}' | rg 'popcrm|a1vb55by4benmh25nd4ga8pt'
107	docker logs --tail=100 <app-container-name>
108	docker exec <app-container-name> wget -qO- http://127.0.0.1/contacts | head
109	docker logs --tail=200 coolify-proxy | rg 'Docker daemon|providerName=docker|error'
110	```
111	
112	If the app responds internally but proxy logs show
113	`Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock`, restart the
114	proxy to restore Traefik route discovery:
115	
116	```bash
117	docker restart coolify-proxy
118	```
119	
120	## Break-glass only (NOT the normal path)
121	
122	Manual Docker on the host is **emergency-only** (CI/CD rules §10). If the CI/CD
123	path is unavailable and production must be restored, you may run the published
124	image directly:
125	
126	```bash
127	docker run -d --name popcrm-web-emergency --network coolify \
128	  ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web:sha-<known-good>
129	```
130	
131	Any emergency action must be followed immediately by restoring the normal
132	Coolify-managed deployment so the server does not become a hidden source of truth.
133

# Deployment — popcrm-web

POP CRM is deployed through the compliant CI/CD path: **GitHub Actions builds
and publishes the image; Coolify pulls and runs it.** GitHub Actions never SSHes
into or mutates the production server.

Shared server/infrastructure standards for the designflow.app VPS live in
[`u2giants/albert-standards/infrastructure`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/tree/main/infrastructure).
When deployment topology, domains, Coolify ownership, server dependencies,
break-glass actions, or infrastructure incident runbooks change here, update that
repository as part of the same documentation pass.

## Normal release path

```text
push to main
  ↓
GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/deploy.yml)
  verify        – npm ci, npm run lint, npm run build (tsc -b && vite build)
  build-and-push – Docker build (build-args: COMMIT_HASH/DATE,
                   VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY,
                   VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN),
                   push to GHCR (tags: latest, main, sha-<sha>)
  deploy         – POST Coolify deploy API, then wait for crm.designflow.app
  ↓
Coolify (https://coolify.designflow.app)
  pulls ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web:latest and updates the container
  ↓
crm.designflow.app  (runtime host only)
```

Every release is traceable from: GitHub Actions run history → the `sha-<commit>`
tag in GHCR → Coolify's deployment history → the repo commit. The running build's
commit + timestamp are also shown in the app header (top bar).

To release: merge/commit to `main`. That's it. Documentation / AI-context-only
changes (`docs/**`, `**/*.md`, `.claudeignore`, `.cursorignore`, `.copilotignore`)
are path-ignored and do not trigger a build.

## Topology (the facts an owner needs to audit / recreate)

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Deployment platform | Coolify `https://coolify.designflow.app` |
| Project | POP Creations CRM (`yp84tp0tmmshhcebgsd4j463`) |
| Environment | production |
| Coolify application | `popcrm-web` — uuid `a1vb55by4benmh25nd4ga8pt` |
| Server (runtime host) | localhost (`onwp0kd7w1w74w9yeotnoihp`) |
| Registry image | `ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web` (public package) |
| Image tag pattern | `latest`, `main`, `sha-<commit-sha>` |
| Production domains | `crm.designflow.app`, `crm-dev.designflow.app` |
| Deploy trigger | GitHub Actions → `POST {COOLIFY_BASE_URL}/api/v1/deploy?uuid=<app>` |
| Runtime config owner | Coolify (domains, ports, restart policy, health) |
| Build-time config | `VITE_*` (baked into the static bundle — see note) |
| Rollback | redeploy a previous `sha-<sha>` image via Coolify |

### GitHub Actions secrets (CI/CD only)

- `COOLIFY_BASE_URL` — `https://coolify.designflow.app`
- `COOLIFY_API_TOKEN` — Coolify API token used to trigger the deploy
- `COOLIFY_SERVER_UUID` — the Coolify **application** uuid (`a1vb55by4benmh25nd4ga8pt`)
- `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` — Supabase project URL baked into the static bundle
- `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` — Supabase public anon key baked into the static
  bundle. This is client configuration, not a service-role key.
- `LOGODEV_TOKEN` — logo.dev **publishable** token, passed as the
  `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` Docker build-arg (`deploy.yml` → Dockerfile) so account
  logos bake into the bundle. **Optional**: if unset the build still succeeds and
  accounts render initials avatars. Client-safe (publishable), not a real secret.

The image is pushed to GHCR with the workflow's built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN`
(`packages: write`); no registry PAT is stored. Because `popcrm-web` is a public
repo, its GHCR package is public, so Coolify pulls it anonymously.

### Runtime config note (static SPA)

This is a static site, so its few `VITE_*` values are **build-time**, baked into
the bundle at `npm run build`. `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` and
`VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` are required by `src/lib/supabase.ts`; changing them
requires rebuilding and redeploying. This is the one documented exception to
"runtime config lives in Coolify" (CI/CD rules §19) — a static bundle has no
server-side runtime env. No service-role keys or backend secrets are baked in.

## Rollback

Preferred (CI/CD rules §6.3): in Coolify, redeploy the `popcrm-web` application
pinned to a previous immutable tag, e.g. `ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web:sha-<old>`.
Do **not** roll back by hand-editing containers or running `docker run` on the host.

## Verify a release

```bash
curl -fsS https://crm.designflow.app | rg -o '<title>[^<]+'      # <title>POP CRM
curl -fsS https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app/health            # Fireflies webhook
# The header in the app shows the deployed commit + NYC time.
```

## Troubleshoot 502 after deploy

On 2026-06-22, a deploy succeeded and the app container was healthy, but
`crm.designflow.app` returned 502 because `coolify-proxy`/Traefik lost access to
the Docker socket and could not discover the new upstream container.

Use this order before rebuilding or rolling back:

```bash
docker ps --format '{{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Status}}' | rg 'popcrm|a1vb55by4benmh25nd4ga8pt'
docker logs --tail=100 <app-container-name>
docker exec <app-container-name> wget -qO- http://127.0.0.1/contacts | head
docker logs --tail=200 coolify-proxy | rg 'Docker daemon|providerName=docker|error'
```

If the app responds internally but proxy logs show
`Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock`, restart the
proxy to restore Traefik route discovery:

```bash
docker restart coolify-proxy
```

## Break-glass only (NOT the normal path)

Manual Docker on the host is **emergency-only** (CI/CD rules §10). If the CI/CD
path is unavailable and production must be restored, you may run the published
image directly:

```bash
docker run -d --name popcrm-web-emergency --network coolify \
  ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web:sha-<known-good>
```

Any emergency action must be followed immediately by restoring the normal
Coolify-managed deployment so the server does not become a hidden source of truth.

content.content:
1	# Architecture — popcrm-web
2	
3	`popcrm-web` is a static React SPA for POP CRM.
4	
5	```txt
6	Browser
7	  -> https://crm.designflow.app
8	  -> popcrm-web nginx container
9	  -> React/Vite SPA
10	  -> Supabase JS client
11	  -> https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co
12	  -> shared Supabase/Postgres backend
13	```
14	
15	Fireflies integration is handled by a separate worker endpoint:
16	
17	```txt
18	Fireflies
19	  -> https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app/s/fireflies-webhook
20	  -> popcrm-fireflies container
21	  -> Supabase CRM tables
22	```
23	
24	Important operational note: the Fireflies `/health` endpoint only proves the
25	webhook server is up. It does not prove Fireflies is delivering webhook events.
26	On 2026-06-14, the CRM backend had 27 `crm_meeting_note` rows with latest meeting
27	date `2026-04-14`, while the Fireflies API listed newer transcripts through
28	`2026-06-11` and worker/proxy logs showed no recent webhook deliveries. Verify
29	stale ingestion from CRM row dates, worker/proxy logs, and the Fireflies
30	dashboard webhook configuration.
31	
32	The frontend has no database and stores no CRM data locally. It uses Supabase
33	Auth in the browser, then loads the user's CRM profile with
34	`api.current_user_profile()`.
35	
36	## Data loading
37	
38	Data loading is page-scoped through TanStack Query hooks in
39	`src/features/crm/queries.ts`. The app no longer bootstraps every CRM collection
40	on mount. Until a screen has real server-side pagination/search/filtering plus
41	true aggregate counts, list queries must load the full relevant dataset through
42	the paged helpers in `api.ts`. A 2026-06-22 TanStack Query refactor briefly used
43	hard-coded 50/100/300 row limits and made production screens look empty even
44	though data was intact. Page-scoped is fine; arbitrary partial slices are not.
45	High-change surfaces such as Email Routing, Tasks, Meetings, and Notes also use
46	conservative background refetch intervals.
47	
48	Reads in `src/features/crm/api.ts` go through browser-safe `api.crm_*` views.
49	Core account/contact writes use guarded RPCs (`api.crm_update_account`,
50	`api.crm_update_contact`); CRM-owned rows write to `crm.*` tables or narrower RPCs.
51	Write operations use mutation hooks in `queries.ts`, with optimistic query-cache
52	updates where the UI already had instant behavior. Related queries are invalidated
53	after writes so joined view rows reconcile with Supabase.
54	
55	Contact writes have one extra wrinkle: CRM relationship attributes
56	(`department`, `contact_type`, `scope`) live on `core.contact_company`, not
57	`core.contact`. The frontend must send the current account when editing those
58	fields so `api.crm_update_contact` can target the correct relationship row. The
59	2026-06-23 migration
60	`shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260623024500_crm_update_contact_clear_relationship_fields.sql`
61	adds explicit `p_clear_*` flags because `null` alone is ambiguous in RPC calls
62	and the old `coalesce` semantics could not clear values. Until that migration is
63	confirmed applied everywhere, `updateBuyer` keeps a fallback call using the old
64	RPC argument shape.
65	
66	Realtime frontend scaffolding lives in `src/features/crm/realtime.ts`. It listens
67	for base-table changes, debounces bursty tables, and invalidates the relevant
68	query keys so the app refetches browser-safe `api.crm_*` views instead of trying
69	to render raw realtime payloads. Supabase publication/RLS enablement is shared-db
70	work and must follow `shared-db/AGENTS.md`.
71	
72	Contacts are intentionally special after the 2026-06-22 incidents. The frontend
73	uses `api.crm_contact_segment_list` for the Cust Contacts, Dept. Contacts, and
74	Triage tabs, and `api.crm_contact_segment_counts` for tab counts. The All tab
75	uses the same segmented view without a segment filter, but it is lazy-loaded only
76	after the user opens All. Do not reintroduce PostgREST `order('name')` or
77	server-side `company_customer_status` filters for contact reads: the previous
78	`security_invoker` view plus derived-field ordering/filtering timed out on the
79	third paged browser request and made Contacts show all zeroes. The durable
80	database fix is
81	`shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260622043000_crm_contact_segments.sql`, which
82	preserves `api.crm_contact_list`, adds the explicit `app.has_app_access('crm')`
83	guard, and exposes the server-computed segment/count contracts.
84	
85	## Key modules
86	
87	| Module | Purpose |
88	|---|---|
89	| `src/auth/auth.tsx` | Supabase Auth session plus CRM profile state |
90	| `src/lib/supabase.ts` | Supabase JS client and Microsoft/Azure OAuth helper |
91	| `src/lib/database.types.ts` | Generated Supabase schema types |
92	| `src/lib/types.ts` | Frontend domain types/adapters used by components |
93	| `src/features/crm/api.ts` | All Supabase view/RPC/table reads and writes |
94	| `src/features/crm/queries.ts` | TanStack Query keys, full/segmented read hooks, mutation hooks, Fireflies health query |
95	| `src/features/crm/realtime.ts` | Debounced Supabase Realtime invalidation scaffold |
96	| `src/features/crm/constants.ts` | Enums, stage lists, tone/status helpers |
97	| `src/features/crm/format.ts` | Display formatters: `label()`, `formatDate()`, `relatedName()`, etc. |
98	| `src/features/crm/useRecordSelection.ts` | URL-param-backed record selection (deep links) |
99	| `src/app/AppLayout.tsx` + `src/app/routes.tsx` | App shell and route-per-page |
100	
101	## Page components (`src/features/crm/pages/`)
102	
103	| Page | Route | Notes |
104	|---|---|---|
105	| `OverviewPage` | `/` | KPI strip, email volume chart, routing donut, pipeline bar, activity panels |
106	| `PipelinePage` | `/pipeline` | Board (kanban by stage) + List (DataTable) toggle |
107	| `AccountsPage` | `/accounts` | DataTable + AccountDrawer. Segmented tabs: **Accounts** (default — hides "Not a Customer"), **Triage** (New Companies awaiting review), **Not a customer**, **All**. Status & Chain cells are inline-editable colored chips |
108	| `DepartmentsPage` | `/departments` | DataTable grouped/sorted by Account. Department-name clicks open `DepartmentDrawer` with assigned contacts and programs |
109	| `ProgramsPage` | `/programs` | DataTable over CRM opportunities/programs + OpportunityModal |
110	| `ContactsPage` | `/contacts` | DataTable + ContactDrawer. Segmented tabs: **Cust Contacts** (linked to Active/Potential account, no department), **Dept. Contacts** (linked to Active/Potential account and department), **Triage** (not linked to a customer account), **All**. Account inline-edit choices are row-aware |
111	| `EmailRoutingPage` | `/email` | Segmented tabs (company/dept/program/triage/admin-only all); ignore-rules sidebar |
112	| `MeetingsPage` | `/meetings` | DataTable + MeetingDrawer |
113	| `NotesPage` | `/notes` | DataTable + NoteDrawer |
114	| `TasksPage` | `/tasks` | Board (kanban by status) + List (DataTable) toggle |
115	| `ApprovalsPage` | `/approvals` | DataTable + ApprovalDrawer |
116	| `SettingsPage` | `/settings` | AI model config |
117	
118	## Shared app components (`src/components/app/`)
119	
120	| Component | Purpose |
121	|---|---|
122	| `AppPage` | Page wrapper with scroll container; `listBar` slot for the top toolbar |
123	| `ListBar` | One-row page header: title · count · optional inline segments · spacer · search · filters · actions; `extra` remains a deliberate second row |
124	| `DataTable` | Sortable table with column visibility, resize (visible separator), reorder, and optional `groupBy` row group headers. Per-column tools in the header: a persistent filter icon → checkbox **value popover** (set filter), and a quick-**search box with value autocomplete**. Optional inline editing: columns with `editOptions` render click-to-edit dropdowns and a spreadsheet **drag-to-copy** fill handle; `editOptions` may be a static array or row-aware function. Edits persist via the `onCellEdit` prop. Columns with `opensDetail` are the only detail-drawer triggers once any column opts in. Popovers/autocomplete use fixed positioning to escape `overflow:hidden` |
125	| `AccountLogo` | Brand logo from logo.dev keyed on `retailer.domain` (token `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN`), falling back to `NameAvatar` initials when no domain/token or on image error |
126	| `DetailDrawer` | Side-sheet shell used by all record drawers |
127	| `MetricCard` | KPI tile with icon, value, label, optional tone/color, onClick |
128	| `StatusBadge` | Inline tone-keyed badge (success/danger/warning/neutral/accent) |
129	| `ChartDonut` | Recharts-backed donut chart with center label |
130	| `ChartHBar` | Horizontal bar chart for pipeline distribution |
131	| `ChartAreaVolume` | Area chart for email ingest vs. routed volume (12-week rolling) |
132	| `CommandSearch` | Global ⌘K search palette |
133	| `FilterSelect` | Dropdown multi-select for column filters in DataTable |
134	| `NameAvatar` | Name-hue-derived circular avatar (no image dependency) |
135	
136	## Domain drawers (`src/features/crm/components/`)
137	
138	Each drawer is a `DetailDrawer` with domain-specific sections. All support deep-linking via `useRecordSelection` (URL search param).
139	
140	| Drawer | Key features |
141	|---|---|
142	| `OpportunityModal` | Full-screen-capable dialog; board card click; Ask AI scroll, Share clipboard, Expand toggle; composer calls `createNote` |
143	| `AccountDrawer` | Logo avatar (`AccountLogo`) in header, colored status/chain chips, contact list, opportunity list, close button footer |
144	| `ContactDrawer` | Related account, opportunity list |
145	| `DepartmentDrawer` | Department detail panel with core fields, all contacts assigned to that department, and all programs assigned to that department |
146	| `EmailDrawer` | Mail-header preview band; `MethodConfidence` bar; Apply routing / Create ignore rule actions |
147	| `MeetingDrawer` | Participant chips (`NameAvatar`), Fireflies transcript tile, action items checklist |
148	| `TaskDrawer` | Clickable status chips, overdue amber callout, assignee avatar |
149	| `NoteDrawer` | Source badge, action items checklist, save button |
150	| `ApprovalDrawer` | Stage select with color-coded status items; licensor comments with left-accent border |
151	
152	## Design tokens
153	
154	All color is OKLCH via CSS custom properties in `src/index.css`:
155	
156	- `--chart-1` … `--chart-5` — chart palette
157	- `--chip-*` — status chip backgrounds/borders/foregrounds
158	- `--shadow-xs` / `--shadow-sm` — card shadows
159	- `--stage-*` — pipeline stage colors
160	- `color-mix(in oklch, …)` — tinted backgrounds derived from token colors
161	
162	No ad-hoc hex or Tailwind palette colors in project-owned code.
163	
164	## Board/list toggle pattern
165	
166	`PipelinePage` and `TasksPage` both implement a board/list toggle:
167	
168	- `view` state: `'board' | 'list'`
169	- Segmented control rendered in `ListBar`'s `actions` slot
170	- Board: kanban columns rendered as a grid of cards
171	- List: `DataTable` with full sort/filter support
172	- `AppPage scroll={view === 'board' ? false : true}` — board manages its own overflow
173	
174	## Density toggle
175	
176	`.density-compact` on `<html>`, toggled via `AppHeader` and persisted to `localStorage`. Compact mode tightens padding/font-size via Tailwind variants.
177
